repositories.brief_summary: GC idle phase landed, String.drop_prefix/suffix added, GADT error messages improved, and major ocamltest overhaul merged.
repositories.new_features_summary: New String.drop_prefix/drop_suffix functions, GC idle phase, improved GADT error messages, and lazy structured constant optimization.
repositories.new_features:
- **`String.drop_prefix` and `String.drop_suffix`** added to the stdlib, returning `Some t` when the string matches the given prefix/suffix or `None` otherwise, mirroring similar functions in Ruby and Elixir ([#14487](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14487))
- **Major GC idle phase** merged: adds an `Idle` phase that allows the GC to skip unnecessary work between cycles, addressing GC pacing and CPU usage regressions reported for OCaml 5.x workloads ([#14365](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14365))
- **Improved GADT error messages** now include hints about type variables introduced by GADT equations when they appear in error output without context (e.g. `$0`, `$1`, `$2`), making type errors involving GADTs more readable ([#14142](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14142))
- **`lazy v` optimization for structured constants** merged: expressions like `lazy (Cons (x, s))` are now optimized at compile time to skip the forward block allocation, achieving ~2.7x speedup in benchmarks compared to OCaml 5.3 ([#13919](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13919))
- **`ocamltest` conditionals** `&&`, `||`, and `if/then/else` added to test statement language, making it possible to write platform-conditional test setup without duplicating blocks ([#14532](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14532))
- **`ocamltest` `system` variable** introduced, exposing the configure-time system name (e.g. `macosx`, `linux`, `freebsd`) for use in test conditions, simplifying platform-specific tests ([#14545](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14545))
- **Inclusion check error messages** improved: when compiling with a ppx that produces intermediate `.pp.ml` files, error messages now reference the original source file rather than the ppx-generated artifact ([#14205](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14205))
- **Memprof status field refactor** replaces the `-Inf` sentinel value for minimal sampling with a dedicated `CONFIG_STATUS_SAMPLING_MIN` enum value, reducing fragility around boxed float dereferences in the memprof trigger path ([#14384](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14384))
- **`caml_startup_exn` removed from native debugger test references** to fix CI flakiness where GCC inlines `caml_startup_exn` into `caml_startup` when building with `--with-pic` ([#14549](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14549))
- **`Load_path.remove_dir` bug fixed** in the toplevel: calling `#remove_directory` previously destroyed the `auto_include_callback`, causing `open Str` to fail after directory manipulation ([#14551](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14551))
- **Manual runtime chapter indentation fix** corrects broken indentation around the `n` and `o` options in the `OCAMLRUNPARAM` documentation section ([#14542](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14542))
- **Git-archive export-ignore** merged: removes testsuite, manual, and other files from GitHub-generated source archives, shrinking opam installation archives by ~25% and saving ~100 seconds on first-time Windows installs ([#14152](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14152))
- **`Scannable_*` macros** replace raw `No_scan_tag` comparisons throughout the runtime to reduce chance of direction/equality errors; also fixes a latent bug in `weak.c` ([#14493](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14493))
- **Globroots test corrected** to use proper OCaml value macros (`Hd_hp`, `Field`) instead of raw pointer casts, fixing a potential TSan false positive ([#14548](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14548))
- **Major GC ephemeron code clarified** with renamed terminology ("rounds" instead of "cycles") and additional comments to match the "Retrofitting Parallelism" paper ([#13629](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13629))
- **FreeBSD frame pointer testsuite** updated to use the new `if/then` ocamltest syntax for conditional flags ([#14530](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14530))
repositories.activity_summary: New PRs opened include atomic arrays API, Fun.todo with location payload, and typing refactors; merged work spans GC, stdlib, and tooling.
repositories.activity:
- **Newly opened: `Atomic.Array` module** proposed by [@clef-men](https://github.com/clef-men) and [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) implementing atomic arrays over atomic record fields machinery, with `make`, `init`, `get`, `set`, `exchange`, `compare_and_set`, and `fetch_and_add` operations; awaiting stdlib API second approval ([#14380](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14380))
- **Newly opened: `Fun.todo` with code location payload** by [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole), adding `Fun.todo : unit -> 'a` backed by a new `Todo of todo` builtin exception with an abstract location type, enabling error messages like `File "foo.ml", line 3` even without backtraces enabled ([#14525](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14525))
- **Newly opened: `Array.fold_left2` and `Array.fold_right2`** proposed by [PetitFrapo](https://github.com/PetitFrapo) to match the existing `List` module equivalents for uniformity ([#14553](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14553))
- **Newly opened: s390x native `Iatomic_fetch_add`** using the `LAAG` instruction by [Zane Hambly](https://github.com/Zaneham), adding TSan instrumentation for the new atomic operation ([#14524](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14524))
- **Newly opened: s390x GOT-indirect calls fix** by [Zane Hambly](https://github.com/Zaneham) to prevent heap corruption when shared runtime `libasmrun_shared.so` PLT trampolines run on the OCaml fiber stack ([#14547](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14547))
- **Newly opened: CFI support for Power architecture** by [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) enabling GDB/profiler backtraces on ppc64le, the last native backend missing CFI information ([#14482](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14482))
- **Newly opened: `Fix Obj.dup` on closures** by [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) to handle closures and infix tags in `caml_obj_with_tag`, upstreamed from oxcaml ([#14492](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14492))
- **Newly opened: typing refactor series** by [Alistair (johnyob)](https://github.com/johnyob) splitting `type_let` into `type_let_nonrec` / `type_let_rec` ([#14394](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14394)), refactoring `type_approx` for level-awareness ([#14538](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14538)), supporting locally-abstract type approximation in `let rec` ([#14517](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14517)), and approximating modular explicits in `let rec` ([#14537](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14537))
- **Newly opened: typechecker error trace refactor** by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) introducing a structured type for error traces to separate post-processing from printing and fix irregular `Method`/`Tag` handling ([#14536](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14536))
- **Newly opened: `LN_S`, `AR`, `ARFLAGS` in configure and Makefiles** by [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA), replacing `ARCMD` with idiomatic GNU Make `AR`/`ARFLAGS` variables ([#14552](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14552))
- **Newly opened: missing minor heap allocations fix** for main domain by [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist), correcting runtime events counters discarded when `CAML_RUNTIME_EVENTS_DESTROY()` runs before `caml_shutdown()` ([#14522](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14522))
- **Ongoing: `Fix PUSHENVACC4` opcode** by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo), found via AI-assisted review of the bytecode emitter: a `n < 4` vs `n <= 4` off-by-one caused the 1-word `PUSHENVACC4` opcode to never be emitted; additional optimizations for `ATOM`/`PUSHATOM` patterns being discussed ([#14534](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14534))
- **Ongoing: `Always keep names`** typing PR by [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) to preserve type variable names through `copy` operations, producing cleaner inferred types like `val map : ('a -> 'b) -> 'a list -> 'b list` rather than `val map : ('a -> 'a0) -> ...` ([#13328](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13328))
- **Ongoing: Haiku native backend** activation PR by [Anarchos](https://github.com/Sylvain78) to enable the native compiler on Haiku OS ([#14138](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14138))
- **Ongoing: warning 16 `unerasable-optional-argument` improvement** by [Alistair (johnyob)](https://github.com/johnyob) to fix directional sensitivity in the check ([#14512](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14512))
- **Ongoing: proposal for nested record definitions** by [David Sancho](https://github.com/davesnx), extending OCaml's inline record mechanism from constructors to record fields; discussion ongoing about type naming and second-class value limitations ([#14527](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14527))
- **Ongoing: WIP labelled modules sync improvement** by [Michael Neumann](https://github.com/mneumann) proposing a Perl script to selectively preserve labels in `sync_stdlib_docs`, following the merge of `String.drop_prefix` ([#14544](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14544))
- **Closed without merge: `WIP: Update operator documentation`** by [Kovacsics Robert](https://github.com/KoviRobi) closed after one year in draft state ([#13697](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13697))
- **Closed without merge: `Fix Complex.sqrt(_, -0.0)`** by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo), found via AI review but determined to be correct behavior per the existing `Complex.sqrt` specification ([#14535](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14535))
- **Closed without merge: `atomic record fields [%atomic.field ...]`** alternative approach PR closed by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) now that `#13404` (`[%atomic.loc ...]`) has merged ([#13707](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13707))
repositories.discussions_summary: Debates on Fun.todo location payload design, GC pacing defaults in 5.5, and nested record field proposals attracted substantial discussion.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **`Fun.todo` API design** generated extended discussion across multiple reviewers on whether to encode the call location in the exception payload: [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) argued for payload inclusion for cases where users see errors without backtraces, [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) wanted an abstract type to avoid committing to a representation, and [Yawar Amin](https://github.com/yawaramin) argued for a simple `Todo of string` with manual `~__LOC__` passing; the PR now uses an abstract `todo` type marked deprecated ([#14525](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14525))
- **GC pacing defaults in OCaml 5.5** discussed after [Romain Beauxis](https://github.com/toots) reported that 5.5 uses significantly more CPU than 4.14 for streaming workloads with large custom blocks; `custom_major_ratio = 250` was found to restore near-4.14 behavior, and the merged GC idle phase PR ([#14365](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14365)) is expected to help further ([#14533](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14533))
- **`let module` value restriction regression** reported by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) in OCaml 5.5 related to PR [#13839](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13839); [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) confirmed it should not break previously-typechecking code and looped in reviewers [Samuel Vivien](https://github.com/samsa1) and [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) ([#14554](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14554))
- **AI-assisted bug finding** highlighted in multiple PRs: [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) used Claude to find the `PUSHENVACC4` off-by-one ([#14534](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14534)) and several other bytecode optimizations, generating discussion on the approach and on test methodology for AI-discovered fixes
- **Readable multi-line quoted string literals** ([#13860](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13860)) saw renewed discussion with [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) proposing `{foo\ ... |foo}` syntax and [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) discussing the `odoc` specification for stripping leading whitespace
- **Floating attributes with `-no-alias-deps` ignored** ([#11867](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11867)) saw a new comment from [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) proposing an `[@@inherit alert]` attribute on module aliases as a potential solution, allowing build systems to propagate floating attributes without reading every module's source
repositories.trends_summary: Ongoing investment in s390x support, AI-assisted code review gaining traction, and major typing infrastructure overhaul underway.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **s390x architecture receiving sustained attention**: three separate PRs this week from [Zane Hambly](https://github.com/Zaneham) ([#14524](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14524), [#14547](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14547), [#14514](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14514)) plus an existing CFI fix PR ([#14500](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14500)) show growing community effort to bring s390x to full feature parity with other native backends
- **AI-assisted code review** becoming visible in the codebase: both the `PUSHENVACC4` fix ([#14534](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14534)) and the (subsequently closed) `Complex.sqrt` fix ([#14535](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14535)) were found and submitted by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) using Claude; this sparked discussion about test methodology for AI-discovered fixes and the value of automated scanning for specific code patterns
- **Typing infrastructure refactoring** gaining momentum with a multi-PR series by [Alistair (johnyob)](https://github.com/johnyob) ([#14394](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14394), [#14538](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14538), [#14517](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14517), [#14537](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14537)) targeting `let rec` approximation and long-standing issue [#14253](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14253), alongside [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron)'s error trace restructuring ([#14536](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14536))
- **`ocamltest` DSL expansion** continuing: the `if/then/else` and `&&`/`||` conditionals merged this week ([#14532](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14532)) plus the new `system` variable ([#14545](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14545)) show a pattern of incrementally improving the test framework to reduce boilerplate in platform-specific tests
- **OxCaml upstream contributions** visible in several PRs: `Scannable_*` macros ([#14493](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14493)), `Fix Obj.dup` on closures ([#14492](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14492)), and inclusion check error improvement ([#14205](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14205)) were all explicitly noted as upstreamed from `oxcaml/oxcaml`

repositories.brief_summary: README configure instructions corrected; new PR to fix PATH precedence in env hook; depexts-at-update PR saw active review.
repositories.new_features_summary: README corrected to distinguish `./configure` vs `./configure --with-vendored-deps` for dependency setup.
repositories.new_features: - Corrected **configure instructions** in README to explicitly distinguish `./configure` (when OCaml dependencies are already installed) from `./configure --with-vendored-deps` (to auto-download dependencies), preventing contributor confusion during builds ([#6858](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6858))
repositories.activity_summary: Two new PRs opened addressing PATH precedence in the env hook and ongoing depexts-at-update work saw review activity.
repositories.activity:
- [Stephen Sherratt](https://github.com/gridbugs) opened [#6859](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6859) to **update PATH in place in the env hook**, fixing a long-standing behaviour where `opam init`'s shell hook would unconditionally prepend the switch bin dir to PATH, overriding user-configured PATH precedence; the change preserves the relative ordering of the opam switch entry within PATH (fixes [#6815](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6815))
- [#6489](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6489) **depexts status check at `opam update` time** by [@arozovyk](https://github.com/arozovyk) continued active review from [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) and [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou), with detailed suggestions on function naming (`is_dir_read_only`), Windows compatibility in sandbox checks, and test output cleanup; the PR carries `PRIORITY` and `AREA: PERFORMANCE` labels (fixes [#6461](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6461), [#4172](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/4172))
- [#6283](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6283) **speedup `opam update` when nothing changed** (WIP draft by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate)) received a minor update, targeting an 8x speedup on subsequent empty updates (fixes [#5553](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/5553))
- [#6858](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6858) **README configure instruction correction** by [Stephen Sherratt](https://github.com/gridbugs) was merged after review from [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate)
repositories.discussions_summary: Discussion on opam switch link breaking existing _opam switches, and renewed interest in installing from standalone opam files.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Matthieu Sozeau](https://github.com/mattam82) reported [#6857](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6857) that **`opam switch link` silently breaks an existing `_opam` switch** when run erroneously on an already-linked switch directory; [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) noted the issue may be related to the already-fixed [#6449](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6449) and requested version confirmation
- [#4227](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/4227) **installing a package directly from a standalone opam file** (with a `url` field pointing to source) received a new comment from [Pierre Villemot](https://github.com/Halbaroth) supporting the feature wish, citing a need to simplify pre-submission testing workflows without committing to a local repository
repositories.trends_summary: Continued focus on performance of opam update and correctness of environment management (PATH, depexts).
repositories.emerging_trends:
- Two open PRs ([#6283](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6283), [#6489](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6489)) and the new [#6859](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6859) reflect a sustained effort to improve **`opam update` and environment hook behaviour**, covering HTTP caching for faster updates, depext staleness detection, and PATH ordering correctness
- The PATH hook PR ([#6859](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6859)) and the `opam switch link` bug ([#6857](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6857)) both point to ongoing attention to **shell environment correctness** as a user-experience priority
repositories.contributors_summary: Stephen Sherratt opened two PRs and had one merged; Kate reviewed PRs and triaged an issue; arozovyk and rjbou iterated on the depexts PR.
repositories.contributors:
- [Stephen Sherratt](https://github.com/gridbugs) opened [#6859](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6859) (PATH env hook fix) and had [#6858](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6858) (README correction) merged after incorporating a suggestion from [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate)
- [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) reviewed [#6858](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6858) with a concrete suggestion and merged it; provided extensive multi-comment review on [#6489](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6489) covering naming, Windows compatibility, test output, and error-message improvements; triaged [#6857](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6857) by cross-referencing the prior fix in [#6449](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6449)
- [@arozovyk](https://github.com/arozovyk) responded to review comments on [#6489](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6489), clarifying design decisions around development artifacts and coordinating next steps with reviewers
- [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) contributed review responses on [#6489](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6489), providing a concrete diff for the `--depext-only` interaction with the disabled-depexts state
- [Matthieu Sozeau](https://github.com/mattam82) filed [#6857](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6857) describing the silent `opam switch link` breakage behaviour

repositories.brief_summary: Merlin config server support extended to non-dune build systems via new environment variables.
repositories.new_features_summary: New env vars OCAMLLSP_PROJECT_BUILD_SYSTEM and OCAMLLSP_PROJECT_ROOT allow configuring ocaml-lsp with any build system.
repositories.new_features:
- Support for **configuring merlin via non-dune build systems** added by [Andrey Popp](https://github.com/andreypopp) ([#1581](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1581)). When `$OCAMLLSP_PROJECT_BUILD_SYSTEM` is set, ocaml-lsp executes `$OCAMLLSP_PROJECT_BUILD_SYSTEM ocaml-merlin` to obtain merlin configuration using `merlin-dot-protocol`, enabling build systems other than dune to serve as configuration providers.
- The new **`$OCAMLLSP_PROJECT_ROOT`** environment variable allows explicitly specifying the project root directory, bypassing automatic detection ([#1581](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1581)).
- The **`$OCAMLLSP_PROJECT_ROOT_MARKERS`** environment variable (comma-separated list of filenames) enables automatic project root detection by walking up the directory hierarchy to find a directory containing one of the specified marker files ([#1581](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1581)).
- When none of the new environment variables are set, ocaml-lsp **falls back to the existing dune-based behaviour**, making this change fully backwards-compatible ([#1581](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1581)).
repositories.activity_summary: PR #1581 merged after month-long review; automated flake.lock update PR remains open.
repositories.activity:
- [Andrey Popp](https://github.com/andreypopp) had the **merlin config server extensibility** PR [#1581](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1581) merged after being open since January 11, touching `merlin_config.ml`, `merlin_config.mli`, and `CHANGES.md`.
- The **automated Nix flake.lock update** PR [#1437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1437) (opened by `github-actions` in January 2025, updated February 14) remains open, updating `merlin5_3` to the February 2026 snapshot and `nixpkgs` to the February 14 NixOS overlay.
- Issue [#857](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/857) — a 2022 feature request from [Emilio Jesús Gallego Arias](https://github.com/ejgallego) asking about **web-worker support** for ocaml-lsp — was closed on February 11, likely addressed or deferred.
repositories.trends_summary: Ongoing work to decouple ocaml-lsp from dune as the sole build system integration point.
repositories.emerging_trends: - The merge of [#1581](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1581) reflects a broader trend of **decoupling ocaml-lsp from dune** as the only supported build system, making the server usable in projects that use custom or alternative build tooling via the `merlin-dot-protocol`.
repositories.contributors_summary: Andrey Popp authored and merged the merlin config server feature; ejgallego's 2022 web-worker issue was closed.
repositories.contributors:

- [Emilio Jesús Gallego Arias](https://github.com/ejgallego) had a long-standing feature request ([#857](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/857)) about web-worker support closed this week.

repositories.brief_summary: Sandbox improvements for tests and Melange, new trace features, memo deadlock fix, and odoc config generation.
repositories.new_features_summary: New --no-recursive flag for dune describe workspace; file-write trace events; odoc-config.sexp generation for with-doc deps.
repositories.new_features:
- New `--no-recursive` flag for `dune describe workspace` limits output to the specified directories rather than recursing into subdirectories, useful for large monorepos ([#13590](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13590))
- **Trace events for file writes**: dune now emits trace events whenever it directly writes a file (e.g. install files, lock files), visible in `dune trace cat` output and useful for build performance analysis ([#13618](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13618))
- **Melange rules sandboxed by default** starting from dune lang 3.22: `(library ..)` and `(melange.emit ..)` stanzas now sandbox JS build rules and runtime asset rules by default, improving build correctness ([#13619](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13619))
- **All test stanza variants sandboxed by default** starting from dune lang 3.22: previously only `(test)` with `expect` was sandboxed; now `(test)`, `(tests)`, and related stanzas are all sandboxed ([#13617](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13617))
- **odoc configuration files generated and installed**: dune now writes `odoc-config.sexp` files for packages that declare `:with-doc` dependencies, enabling cross-package documentation builds with odoc ([#13476](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13476), closes [#13485](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13485))
- Fixed **deadlock in the `memo` library** when dependency cycles are detected: nodes in `Computing` state with unfilled ivars now receive a `Cancelled` value rather than blocking waiting fibers indefinitely ([#13625](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13625))
- Fixed **sandboxed builds of `(library (stdlib (modules_before_stdlib ..)))`**: ocamldep results now include implicit dependencies on `modules_before_stdlib` modules so sandboxed stdlib library builds no longer fail with missing file errors ([#13624](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13624))
- Fixed **`(dir ..)` field on package declarations** being ignored when assigning cram tests to packages: `dune build @install -p pkg` was broken in the presence of cram tests with package-scoped `dir` stanzas ([#13581](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13581))
- Fixed **`dune trace cat --follow`** exiting prematurely: it previously exited on any `config/exit` event including those from subprocess action traces; it now correctly waits for the main dune process exit event ([#13607](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13607))
- New **`Stdune.At_exit`** module providing ordered shutdown hooks, ensuring the trace file is flushed before the global lock is released and before the digest cache is dumped ([#13608](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13608), [#13609](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13609), [#13605](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13605))

repositories.discussions_summary: Discussion on 3.21.1 release branch merge strategy; debate on how broadly to detect dune-built packages in lockfiles.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **3.21.1 release branch merge strategy** ([#13557](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13557)): [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder), [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter), and [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) debated whether to merge the release branch back into main. The team settled on merging only the changelog entries (via [#13627](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13627)) rather than a full merge, avoiding merge conflicts and preserving clean history for the patch release tag
- **Scope of dune-build detection in lockfiles** ([#13592](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13592)): [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) raised concerns that exact-match detection of dune-generated build commands is too fragile — packages that omit `@doc`, add `-j1`, or call dune differently would not be recognized. [Stephen Sherratt](https://github.com/gridbugs) acknowledged the constraint and discussed whether dune should instead annotate generated opam files with a metadata field
- **OCaml alerts in wrapped libraries** ([#13622](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13622)): a user reported that `[@@@alert ...]` attributes on modules are not propagated through dune's generated alias modules in wrapped libraries. [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) confirmed this is a known upstream OCaml bug ([ocaml/ocaml#11867](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11867)) with no dune-side fix currently possible
- **ocamlbuild sandbox stale file issue** ([#13467](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13467)): [Ambre Austen Suhamy](https://github.com/ElectreAAS) and [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) confirmed the original reproduction case no longer works and updated it; the fix depends on merging a relocatable ocamlbuild patch from [#13229](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13229)
repositories.trends_summary: Sandboxing is being extended to more rule types by default; the trace/observability subsystem is receiving active investment.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Sandboxing as the default**: the pattern of enabling sandboxing by default for new dune language versions is expanding — test stanzas ([#13617](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13617)) and Melange rules ([#13619](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13619)) both moved to sandbox-by-default in 3.22, continuing the trend from earlier cycles
- **Trace and observability investment**: the `dune trace` subsystem received several fixes and a new file-write event type this week ([#13618](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13618), [#13607](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13607), [#13604](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13604), [#13605](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13605), [#13608](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13608)), suggesting ongoing investment in build observability tooling
- **js_of_ocaml incrementality**: three PRs from [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) ([#13611](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13611), [#13612](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13612), [#13613](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13613)) form a coordinated effort to make jsoo builds more incremental by moving from upfront rule generation to demand-driven approaches and per-compilation-unit JS artifacts
- **In-process dependency builds**: [#13592](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13592) represents early exploration of building opam packages in the same dune process rather than shelling out, which could reduce build overhead for pure-dune dependency trees

repositories.brief_summary: Discussion activity on RFC #59 for `val` declarations in structures, with community feedback on shadowing semantics.
repositories.activity_summary: RFC #59 on `val` declarations in structures received new comments debating shadowing rules and their implications for bidirectional type checking.
repositories.activity: - **RFC [#59](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/59)** (`val` declarations in structures), opened by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) in November 2025, received new discussion this week around the proposed semantics for `val` declarations, particularly the rules governing **shadowing** of `val` by subsequent `let` bindings or `include`s ([#59](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/59))
repositories.discussions_summary: Debate on RFC #59 centres on whether `val` declarations should be shadowable and the implications for bidirectional type propagation.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan) raised concerns about the **shadowing rules** for `val` declarations in [#59](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/59), noting that the RFC is ambiguous about whether `val` can be shadowed by a plain `let` or `include`, and arguing that **disallowing shadowing** would make bidirectional type propagation from signatures to module implementations practical
- [Nicolas Jeannerod](https://github.com/Niols) responded in [#59](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/59) with the view that `val` declarations should not be shadowable, interpreting them as attached to the last binding of that name, while remaining open to the alternative interpretation where `val` acts as a simple type annotation with normal shadowing semantics
repositories.trends_summary: Community interest in `val` declarations centres on enabling bidirectional type checking between `.ml` and `.mli` files.
repositories.emerging_trends: - The discussion on [#59](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/59) reflects a broader interest in **bidirectional type checking** for OCaml modules: contributors see `val` declarations in structures as a stepping stone toward propagating type information from `.mli` signatures down into `.ml` implementations, which would extend OCaml's existing constructor disambiguation and type propagation capabilities to the module level
repositories.contributors_summary: Gabriel Scherer authored RFC #59; Stephen Dolan and Nicolas Jeannerod contributed design feedback on shadowing semantics.
repositories.contributors:
- [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) is the author of [#59](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/59), which proposes `val` declarations in structures and includes worked code examples covering local type annotations, polymorphic `val`, and wildcard `val` forms
- [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan) contributed a detailed comment on [#59](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/59) analysing the interaction between shadowing rules and future bidirectional type checking of modules against signatures
- [Nicolas Jeannerod](https://github.com/Niols) contributed a follow-up comment on [#59](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/59) offering a concrete position on the shadowing question and the appropriate semantics for `val` bindings

repositories.brief_summary: Extensive tutorial and documentation quality improvements alongside scraper infrastructure enhancements.
repositories.new_features_summary: Planet scraper now generates structured commit messages and PR bodies with `--commit-file`/`--report-file` CLI flags.
repositories.new_features:
- The **OCaml Planet scraper** gained a new `Scrape_report` OCaml module that returns structured results (`New_post`, `Video_update`, `Error`) instead of printing to stdout. The scraper CLI now accepts `--commit-file` and `--report-file` optional flags to write formatted output files, enabling descriptive scrape PR bodies listing new posts and broken feeds ([#3503](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3503)).
- **Scrape workflow** was updated to use the new structured commit messages and PR bodies, so automated scrape PRs now display a readable summary of what was scraped and any broken feeds rather than a generic placeholder message ([#3503](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3503)).
- **Scheduled scraping workflows** now skip execution on repository forks via an `if: github.event_name != 'schedule' || github.repository == 'ocaml/ocaml.org'` guard, preventing unnecessary CI failures and email notifications on forks while keeping manual dispatch available ([#3508](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3508)).
- **Thomas Letan's blog** feed URL was updated to an OCaml-specific RSS feed, improving the relevance of content appearing on OCaml Planet ([#3497](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3497)).

repositories.activity:
- **Newly opened PRs awaiting review**: [Bozhidar Batsov](https://github.com/bbatsov) has three open cleanup PRs — typo and lint fixes in root-level and developer docs ([#3493](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3493)), lint fixes across exercise files including a file rename (`084_minimal_splanning_tree.md` → `084_minimal_spanning_tree.md`) ([#3494](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3494)), and lint fixes across smaller data directories ([#3495](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3495)), all awaiting rebase and final review after [#3500](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3500) landed.
- **Emacs editor setup tutorial** update opened by [Bozhidar Batsov](https://github.com/bbatsov) ([#3509](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3509)) cleans up the Emacs section, streamlines mode recommendations toward Eglot + ocamllsp, and reduces mentions of legacy modes; under active review discussion with [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac).
- **Automated platform releases PR** ([#3498](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3498)) opened by `github-actions` adding 28 lines of new platform release data is open and awaiting merge.
- **Internationalization initiative** continues with two draft/open PRs by [mt_caret](https://github.com/mt-caret): the foundational i18n infrastructure adding language frontmatter and a language selector ([#3238](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3238)) and an extension adding more Japanese translations of getting-started pages ([#3489](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3489), draft); [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) raised sustainability questions about maintaining translations long-term in the discussion.
- **Neovim plugin installation instructions** update ([#3421](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3421)) by [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) remains open with merge conflicts; [Bozhidar Batsov](https://github.com/bbatsov) suggested pointing directly to the upstream `ocaml.nvim` docs to avoid drift.
- **Suggestion for module TOC** filed by [Arthur Carcano](https://github.com/krtab) ([#3506](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3506)) proposes adding a clickable table of contents for functions in module documentation pages, similar to the sidebar navigation on docs.rs.
- **Comprehensive tutorial quality sweep** merged: [Bozhidar Batsov](https://github.com/bbatsov) fixed ~100 typos and grammar issues across 41 tutorial files ([#3491](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3491)) and then resolved all 469 markdownlint violations across tutorials including adding code block language specifiers, fixing heading levels, and improving link text ([#3492](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3492)).
- **Mechanical markdown lint batch** extracted and merged by [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac) ([#3500](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3500)): one commit per lint rule (MD025/MD001 heading levels across 96 files, MD009 trailing whitespace across 13 files, MD047 missing final newlines across 117 files), co-authored with [Bozhidar Batsov](https://github.com/bbatsov).
- **Dead code removal** by [Bozhidar Batsov](https://github.com/bbatsov) ([#3496](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3496)) removed unused `Url.blog_post` function and a stale `planet-local-blogs` source_tree dependency from the dune build.
- **Small grammar fix** in the language section merged by [José Lopes](https://github.com/joserlopes) ([#3505](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3505)), correcting "this correspond" to "these correspond" for plural agreement.
repositories.discussions_summary: Active debate about AI-assisted PR review workflows and sustainable processes for handling large automated PRs.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **Agentic PR review workflow** discussion emerged in [#3496](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3496): [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac) raised the challenge of reviewing large batches of machine-assisted changes and proposed requiring one narrow category of change per PR with a machine-verifiable check script in the PR description; [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) and [Bozhidar Batsov](https://github.com/bbatsov) offered different perspectives on whether per-lint-rule granularity is necessary for changes that are human-straightforward to review.
- **Exercise page custom rendering concern** surfaced during the lint discussion: [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) noted that ood-gen uses a regex `Str.regexp {|#[ ]*Solution|}` to split exercise markdown, and heading demotion could break this; [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac) verified that `## Solution` still matches the unanchored regex, confirmed in [#3500](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3500).
- **Translation sustainability** debated in [#3238](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3238): [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) noted that without a dedicated translation team, translated docs go stale quickly and maintainers face review decisions in languages they don't know; [mt_caret](https://github.com/mt-caret) proposed periodic LLM-assisted translation update PRs with a human Japanese reviewer as point person.
repositories.trends_summary: AI-assisted contributions are increasing, prompting process discussions about review workflows and PR granularity.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **AI-assisted contributions** are visibly increasing: multiple PRs from [Bozhidar Batsov](https://github.com/bbatsov) carry the `agentic-pr` label and [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac) introduced the label this week, reflecting an emerging norm of tagging AI-generated work for appropriate review handling ([#3500](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3500), [#3503](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3503), [#3508](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3508)).
- **Documentation quality tooling** is gaining investment: the week saw `markdownlint-cli2` applied across the full tutorial corpus, lint config updated to allow siblings-only duplicate headings, and an `agentic-pr` workflow established — suggesting ongoing automation of content quality checks ([#3491](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3491), [#3492](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3492), [#3493](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3493), [#3494](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3494), [#3495](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3495)).
- **Internationalization groundwork** is progressing with two Japanese translation PRs in flight ([#3238](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3238), [#3489](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3489)), though sustainability questions around long-term maintenance remain open.
repositories.contributors_summary: Five contributors active this week spanning code, documentation, content, and review.

repositories.brief_summary: Active week with new package releases including dune 3.21.1, menhir 20260209, yocaml 3.0.0, mirage-crypto 2.0.3, and a large archival of unmaintained packages.
repositories.new_features_summary: New packages added: mkernel 0.0.1 (unikernel with Miou), utcp 0.0.1 (TCP in OCaml), virtfs 1.0.0 (abstract file paths), yocaml 3.0.0 (14 packages, static site generator).
repositories.new_features:
- **mkernel 0.0.1** released by [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) — an initial public release of a unikernel using Miou providing I/O for Solo5/Unikraft targets ([#29361](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29361))
- **utcp 0.0.1** released by [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) — initial public release of a pure OCaml TCP (Transmission Control Protocol) implementation ([#29359](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29359))
- **virtfs 1.0.0** released by [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) — first release exposing modules `Path` for abstracting file paths, `Tree` for describing an abstract file tree, and `Tree.Simple` for a simple file system implementation ([#29381](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29381))
- **yocaml 3.0.0** (14 packages) released by [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) — major version of the YOCaml static site generator, switching from `int` to `float` for time/mtime, adding `Action.write_files`, `Data.Validation.req`/`opt`, and improved validation error pretty-printing ([#29377](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29377))
- **dune 3.21.1** (17 packages) released by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) — patch release fixing build issues on NetBSD and OpenBSD, fixing `melange.emit` package mask behavior, and stopping RPC server from starting on `dune promote` ([#29374](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29374))
- **menhir 20260209** (5 packages) released by [François Pottier](https://github.com/fpottier) — new snapshot release fixing Windows installation issues (the prior 20260203 release contained an `aux.ml` filename invalid on Windows) ([#29364](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29364), [#29365](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29365))
- **mirage-crypto 2.0.3** (7 packages) released by [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) — adds `mirage-crypto-rng-mkernel` and PPC macros support ([#29368](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29368))
- **trace 0.11** (4 packages) and **tracy-client 0.7** released by [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) — trace 0.11 is a major rework of the collector using an open sum type for spans, removing global state, and adding extensible metrics; tracy-client 0.7 updates to Tracy v0.13.1 ([#29345](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29345), [#29346](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29346))
- **grace 0.3.0** released by [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob) — diagnostics library with breaking changes: removes `base`/`core` dependency, replaces `Source.reader` with `Source.Reader.t`, adds line breaks in large diagnostics and TTY detection ([#29379](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29379))
- **containers 3.18** (2 packages) released by [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) — includes breaking changes to `CCListLabel` (named arguments for `compare`, `equal`, `init`), `CCFloat.{min,max}` semantics aligned with Stdlib for NaN, and `CCInt.rem` negative number behavior ([#29372](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29372))
- **multipart_form 0.8.0** (5 packages) released by [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) — fixes eio support and updates to ocamlformat 0.28.1 ([#29367](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29367))
- **miou 0.5.3** and **miou 0.5.4** released by [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) — 0.5.3 fixes signal handling and transfer to dom0; 0.5.4 fixes macOS support and the return value of `poll(3P)` ([#29378](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29378), [#29400](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29400))
- **lintcstubs-arity 0.5.1** released by [Török Edwin](https://github.com/edwintorok) — adds OCaml >= 5.2 support for the C binding header generator ([#29371](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29371))
- **ocp-index 1.4.1** (2 packages) released by [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) — adds support for OCaml 5.5 ([#29358](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29358))
- **grenier 0.16** released by [Frédéric Bour](https://github.com/let-def) — adds `is_singleton` for OCaml 5.5 compatibility and rank-based element access functions ([#29349](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29349))
- **bogue 20260208** released by [珊瑚](https://github.com/sanette) — new version of the SDL2-based GUI library ([#29351](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29351))
- **camlzip 1.14** released by [Xavier Leroy](https://github.com/xavierleroy) — fixes local file header errors for files > 4 GB ([#29352](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29352))
- **smtml 0.21.0** released by [@filipeom](https://github.com/filipeom) — SMT solver frontend with type-safe API for uninterpreted functions and updated maintenance intent ([#29357](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29357))
- **symex 0.1** released by [@redianthus](https://github.com/redianthus) — initial release of symbolic execution library ([#29356](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29356))
- **oktree 0.2.2** released by [Paul Garner](https://github.com/anentropic) — simple octree implementation for nearest-neighbor search in 3D space ([#29376](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29376))
- **passage 0.3.3** released by [José Nogueira](https://github.com/thatportugueseguy) — restores macOS availability for the shared secrets management tool ([#29383](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29383))
- **spectrum 0.7.0** released by [Paul Garner](https://github.com/anentropic) — terminal color and formatting library using OCaml Format module semantic tags ([#29382](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29382))
- **frama-c-lannotate 0.2.5** released by Frama-CI Bot via [Frama-CI Bot](https://github.com/frama-ci-bot) ([#29344](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29344))
repositories.activity_summary: Newly opened PRs include js_of_ocaml 6.3.0/6.3.2, windtrap testing framework, ortools 9.15.0, conf-zlib/bzip2/eigen3 virtual packages, and an ongoing immutable policy discussion.
repositories.activity:
- **js_of_ocaml 6.3.0** (8 packages) opened by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) — a substantial release with wasm number unboxing, reference unboxing, constant sinking pass, JS-parser improvements (decorators, `using` resource management, HTML comments), and many bug fixes including parallel renaming and purity of comparison functions; awaiting resolution of compatibility issues ([#29348](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29348))
- **js_of_ocaml 6.3.2** (8 packages) opened by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) to replace [#29388](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29388), fixing shell completion file installation; labeled "consider for merge" ([#29393](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29393))
- **windtrap 0.1.0** new all-in-one OCaml testing framework opened by [Thibaut Mattio](https://github.com/tmattio) — unifies unit tests, property-based tests, snapshot tests, and expect tests under a single PPX-enabled API with CLI test runner, filter, and bisect_ppx coverage integration ([#29387](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29387))
- **ortools 9.15.0** (2 packages) opened by [Timothy Bourke](https://github.com/tbrk) — Google OR-Tools OCaml interface rewritten in C++ to avoid unnecessary copies and allow feasible solution observers; accompanied by new virtual packages conf-bzip2 and conf-eigen3 and updates to conf-zlib for broader platform support ([#29380](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29380), [#29394](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29394), [#29395](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29395), [#29396](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29396))
- **make the immutable policy stricter** ([#29072](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29072)) by [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) — ongoing policy discussion about tightening repository immutability rules, with [Marcello Seri](https://github.com/mseri) suggesting merge and handling pin-depends separately
- **frama-clang Version 0.0.19** ([#29309](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29309)) by [Basile Desloges](https://github.com/zilbuz) and [Virgile Prevosto](https://github.com/vprevosto) — ongoing work resolving conf-libclang distribution detection (opensuse vs. open-suse as os-distribution vs. os-family)
- **dependency compatibility fixes** merged this week: upper bound on `containers` 3.18 for `decoders` and `ppx_cstubs` by [Marcello Seri](https://github.com/mseri) ([#29391](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29391)), upper bound `lwt < 6.1.0` for `timmy-lwt` ([#29390](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29390)), upper bounds on `trace` revdeps including `fuseau` ([#29347](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29347), [#29370](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29370))
- **package archival** — two large archival PRs merged by [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm): archival of `available: false` packages (removing 594 files, [#29360](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29360)) and archival of packages with `x-maintenance-intent: none` mainly tezos/octez packages (removing 45,238 files across 1,197 packages, [#29363](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29363)); the broader draft archival PR [#29058](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29058) was closed pending further policy discussion
- **bogue dependency version constraint updates** by [珊瑚](https://github.com/sanette) for the API changes introduced in bogue 20260208, restricting and updating dependent package version ranges ([#29353](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29353), [#29354](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29354))
- **primavera repository move** to the `cargocut` organization by [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) ([#29362](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29362))
- **testcontainers-ocaml 0.1.1** (11 packages) submitted by [Sachin Beniwal](https://github.com/benodiwal) — Docker container integration testing for OCaml with modules for Elasticsearch, Kafka, LocalStack, Memcached, MockServer, MongoDB, MySQL, PostgreSQL, RabbitMQ, and Redis; closed needing reporter action ([#29340](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29340))
- **shibboleth 0.0.4** (SML-OCaml converter) by [Asher Frost](https://github.com/wizard7377) closed with many CI failures ([#29256](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29256))
- **quickterface 0.1.0** (quick terminal/web app interfaces) by [Paul D'Souza](https://github.com/ofsouzap) submitted and closed multiple times during iterative fixes ([#29384](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29384), [#29385](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29385), [#29386](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29386), [#29397](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29397), [#29398](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29398))
- **opam custom-install compatibility issue** opened by [Alina Banerjee](https://github.com/alinab) — `opam-custom-install` fails with opam 2.1 against a 2.2 root, identified as needing `available: opam-version >= x & opam-version < y` constraints ([#29369](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29369))
repositories.discussions_summary: Notable discussions around js_of_ocaml 6.3.x compatibility issues, containers 3.18 breaking changes causing revdep failures, and the ongoing archival process policy.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **js_of_ocaml compatibility threads** on [#29348](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29348) revealed that `timmy-lwt` breaks with the new `lwt` 6.1.0 and that `data-encoding` tests fail due to NaN comparison using `=` instead of `compare`; [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) noted these failures are pre-existing and unrelated to the js_of_ocaml release, coordinating with [Marcello Seri](https://github.com/mseri) and [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) on the merge timeline
- **containers 3.18 breaking changes** ([#29372](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29372)) prompted discussion about downstream impact: `CCResult.result` was removed causing `decoders` failures, and a stack overflow was found in `containers.pvec` tests under property-based testing which [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) fixed by addressing a size explosion in qcheck
- **archival policy discussion** around [#29058](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29058) closed, with [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) splitting the work into two less controversial PRs: archiving `available: false` packages and archiving packages with `x-maintenance-intent: none` (primarily tezos/octez), while noting the broader policy needs further discussion
- **OCaml installation on Windows** issue [#29093](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29093) received clarification from [Andre](https://github.com/maroneze) that menhir demo symlink warnings are harmless on Linux/Windows and are caused by Windows symlink limitations in zip archives
repositories.trends_summary: Strong archival and maintenance activity combined with new package submissions in concurrency (miou, mkernel, utcp), tooling (dune, menhir, ocp-index), and web/JS compilation (js_of_ocaml).
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Robur Coop ecosystem expansion**: [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) published three packages in quick succession — `mkernel` (unikernel I/O with Miou for Solo5/Unikraft), `miou` (two patch releases 0.5.3 and 0.5.4 fixing signal handling and macOS poll(3P) issues), and `multipart_form` 0.8.0 with eio support fixes, reflecting active development of the MirageOS/Robur toolchain ([#29361](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29361), [#29378](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29378), [#29400](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29400), [#29367](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29367))
- **OCaml 5.5 compatibility work**: multiple packages adding OCaml 5.5 support, including `grenier 0.16` (adding `is_singleton` for 5.5 compatibility, [#29349](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29349)) and `ocp-index 1.4.1` ([#29358](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29358)), indicating ongoing preparation for the next OCaml release
- **Breaking change coordination**: Several releases this week included breaking API changes (containers 3.18, grace 0.3.0, trace 0.11) requiring coordinated revdep upper bound additions by maintainers, reflecting the ongoing challenge of managing ecosystem-wide API evolution
- **Large-scale repository cleanup**: The archival of thousands of unmaintained packages (primarily tezos/octez packages marked `x-maintenance-intent: none`) represents a concerted effort to keep the repository focused on actively-maintained software, with companion archival repository ([ocaml/opam-repository-archive](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository-archive)) receiving the archived packages
repositories.contributors_summary: 23 unique contributors participated with releases, reviews, maintenance fixes, and archival work across the OCaml ecosystem.
repositories.contributors:

repositories.brief_summary: Two patch releases (6.3.1, 6.3.2) shipped to fix shell completion install, with compiler performance and internal API improvements.
repositories.new_features_summary: Compiler internal Set module gains compare_cardinal_with and to_list_bounded APIs; BitSet next_free/next_mem rewritten with CTZ for faster iteration.
repositories.new_features:
- New **`Set.compare_cardinal_with`** function added to the compiler's internal `stdlib.ml`, providing O(min(n, cardinal s)) cardinality comparison instead of O(cardinal s), enabling early-exit comparisons against a threshold ([#2180](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2180), [2aa2def](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/2aa2def58e4cd3e4cf45f618e16388263c4e52e5))
- New **`Set.to_list_bounded`** function added to the compiler's `stdlib.ml`, returning a sorted element list only if the set has at most N elements, traversing at most N+1 nodes, useful for bounded-cardinality checks ([2aa2def](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/2aa2def58e4cd3e4cf45f618e16388263c4e52e5))
- **`BitSet.next_free` and `BitSet.next_mem`** rewritten using a **count-trailing-zeros (CTZ)** approach, scanning word-at-a-time instead of bit-by-bit, substantially reducing time for sparse/dense bitsets ([#2179](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2179), [4e928f7](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/4e928f799283d20f2e83e0e09ba11a406f2fbec8))
- **`Dgraph.Make` functor** relaxed to accept custom `SET` and `MAP` module types instead of requiring the full `Set.S` and `Map.S` signatures, enabling use with the faster internal set/map implementations ([e03abe9](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/e03abe96ded3c8037a5ace3c874fdf88cc43a973))
- **Shell completion files** (bash/zsh/fish) now correctly install under monorepo/workspace builds, fixing a regression introduced by dune's handling of generated files ([#2186](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2186), [282427e](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/282427ee2590187bfceb6b6470f2ba39072c7b69))
repositories.activity_summary: Active week of compiler performance work merged and two patch releases shipped to fix shell completion installation regressions.

repositories.trends_summary: Sustained focus on compiler internal performance: set/map data structure optimizations and bit manipulation improvements across multiple PRs.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Compiler performance micro-optimizations** are a consistent theme: this week brought BitSet word-scanning with CTZ ([#2179](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2179)), Set.cardinal early-exit ([#2180](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2180)), Set shortcircuit ops ([#2182](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2182)), and the upcoming subst speedup ([#2185](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2185)), suggesting a broader pass to reduce compiler overhead
- **Variable coalescing** ([#2166](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2166)) and **constant sinking** show that output code size and V8 register pressure are active optimization targets, with benchmarks showing 74–91% register reduction and up to 16% compressed output size reduction
- **Wasm backend expansion** continues through several long-running draft PRs (WASI runtime, Stack Switching effects, Bigstring optimizations, OXCaml support), reflecting sustained multi-month effort on broadening the Wasm target's capabilities
- **Release cadence** for patch fixes is fast: two releases in two days (6.3.1 and 6.3.2) to resolve a monorepo build regression, indicating active responsiveness to downstream ecosystem issues like [ocaml/dune#13500](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13500)

repositories.brief_summary: Active week with 34 merged PRs spanning SIMD/arm64 improvements, abstract kinds, chamelon tooling enhancements, runtime fiber/GC fixes, and build system updates.
repositories.new_features_summary: New features include abstract kinds, small-int SIMD intrinsics, [%%expect_asm] test support, chamelon subcommands, manual module init, and -dfexpr-annot compiler flag.
repositories.new_features:
- **Abstract kinds part 2** merges basic abstract kinds and kind aliases into the type system, allowing kind declarations with manifests and kind aliases in module signatures; environment arguments are now threaded through jkind operations ([#5071](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5071))

- **Indexing and arraylike primitives for small integers** adds `%caml_bytes_geti8`, `%caml_bytes_geti16`, and related indexed-by primitives supporting `int8` and `int16#` indices for arrays, strings, bigstrings, and bytes, including sign-extended variants not available in upstream ([#4779](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4779))

- **`int16` x86 intrinsics** `caml_popcnt_int16`, `caml_lzcnt_int16`, and `caml_bmi_tzcnt_int16` added to the SIMD backend, with simdgen extended to support instructions encoded with multiple legacy prefixes ([#5407](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5407))

- **Small int `ctz`, `clz`, `popcnt`** primitives added for `int8` and `int16` unboxed types ([#5393](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5393))

- **`[%%expect_asm]` support** added to expect tests, enabling verification of generated x86_64 assembly output with architecture-tagged expectations (`X86_64{|...|}`), a new `only-default-codegen` test action, and assembly capture infrastructure in the emitter pipeline ([#5350](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5350))

- **`chamelon run` subcommand** introduced as the new canonical interface; the old top-level `chamelon -c '...' file.ml` syntax still works but shows a deprecation warning nudging users toward `chamelon run -c '...' file.ml` ([#5421](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5421))

- **chamelon dune integration** adds `chamelon dune` subcommand plus `minimize-failure` and `minimize-stdlib` Makefile targets; it parses `dune build --debug-dependency-path` and `dune show rules` output to automatically minimize compilation failures in dune projects ([#5423](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5423))

- **Manual module (compilation unit) initialization** via new `-manual-module-init` linker flag; the C functions `caml_init_module`, `caml_init_module_exn`, and `caml_init_module_from_ocaml` allow on-demand module initialization with dependency tracking, useful for large `output-complete-obj` objects ([#5395](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5395))

- **`-dfexpr-annot` compiler flag** dumps flambda2 fexpr representations alongside `.cmx` files at multiple pipeline stages (`raw`, `simplify`, `reaper`), similar to how `-bin-annot` produces `.cmt` files ([#5210](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5210))

- **`let poly_` and `val poly_` syntax parsing** support added to the parser for layout-polymorphic bindings ([#5392](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5392))

- **`runtime5` added to `ocamlc -config` output** so downstream tooling can detect the runtime variant ([#5415](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5415))

- **`configure` now checks required tool versions** with clear error messages for wrong OCaml, dune, or menhir versions; a `--disable-optional-checks` flag allows skipping version checks ([#5348](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5348))

- **`oxcaml-dev` opam package** simplifies development switch setup to three `opam` commands; future tool requirement changes only need a version bump in `oxcaml-dev.opam` ([#5344](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5344))

- **Wide vectors (vec256) translated to pairs on arm64** using two `vec128` operations in the Lambda IR, enabling most 256-bit SIMD tests on arm without AVX; two new primitives for splitting and joining vec256 work on both targets ([#5291](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5291))

- **Bit matrix for IRC interference graph** selectable via `-regalloc-param BIT_MATRIX_THRESHOLD:k`; uses `O(n²/8)` memory and better cache locality for functions with fewer registers than the threshold ([#5296](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5296))

- **`prefetchr` instead of `prefetchw`** in the major GC fixes incorrect exclusive cache-line locking that made multi-domain programs referencing shared objects over 2× slower ([#5416](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5416))

- **Boxed vectors use tag 0** on amd64, matching arm64 representation and removing the `Abstract_tag`; the `caml_copy_vec128` family of inlined allocators now uses `caml_alloc_small_with_reserved` ([#5410](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5410))

- **Script to build individual files** (`tools/build_file.sh`) allows compiling a single `.ml` or `.mli` file with `dune`, even when dependencies don't fully build, useful for resolving merge conflicts independently ([#5404](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5404))

- **Fiber caches now return fibers to the allocating domain** by tracking `domain_idx` in `stack_info`; new `caml_alloc_stack_caches` / `caml_enable_stack_caches` / `caml_disable_stack_caches` API replaces the old per-domain pointer ([#5363](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5363))

- **`caml_failure_exn` added to public runtime API** in `runtime/caml/fail.h` for constructing `Failure` exception values from C without raising ([#5395](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5395))

- **fexpr testsuite actions** added via ocamltest integration (`dump-raw-fexpr`, `dump-simplify-fexpr`, `dump-reaper-fexpr`, `check-fexpr-dump`, `run-fexpr`), enabling `.fl` flambda2 files in the testsuite for middle-end testing ([#5413](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5413))

- **`Format_doc` backports** from upstream OCaml: `deprecated`/`deprecated1` helpers ([#5381](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5381)), `Doc.iter` initialization fix ([#5380](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5380)), and `report_error_doc` alongside `report_error` for compiler-libs users ([#5378](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5378))
repositories.activity_summary: Newly opened PRs include dune integration for chamelon, preemption/tick-thread work, upstream 5.4 merge conflict resolution, and several type-system extensions.
repositories.activity:
- **New PRs opened** this week include chamelon dune integration by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) ([#5423](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5423)), framework for static computations in the compiler by [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr) ([#5430](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5430)), type-level eval in type expressions by [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski) ([#5402](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5402)), redundant modality warning by [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) ([#5373](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5373)), and `.mli` conflict resolution for the 5.4 merge by [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon) ([#5426](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5426))

- Ongoing **preemption work** by [Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark) continues with the initial preemption implementation ([#4881](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4881)) and tick thread refactoring ([#5349](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5349)); a race fix for `requested_external_interrupt` initialization was merged ([#5424](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5424))

- **Upstream OCaml 5.4 merge** progresses with a large draft PR covering the full merge ([#5325](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5325)); build system conflicts were resolved ([#5376](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5376)) and missing dynlink prerequisites merged ([#5403](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5403)); a backport emancipating dynlink from compilerlibs is open for review ([#5420](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5420))

- **CFG backend invariants** expanded across the week with terminator arity checks ([#5388](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5388)), basic instruction arity checks ([#5390](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5390)), liveness-at-entry checks ([#5375](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5375)), destruction-point synchronization ([#5182](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5182)), and a new arm64 CI job ([#5411](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5411)), all by [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc)

- **Mode/modality type system** work includes functors affected by modalities ([#5371](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5371)), modules must be `aliased many` ([#5364](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5364)), warn on imprecise kind annotations ([#5362](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5362)), store jkinds in typedast ([#5417](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5417)), and `Sort.set` interface simplification ([#5419](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5419)) by [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) and [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn)

- **DWARF/debugging** work has multiple open PRs: CFI information tests revealing existing bugs in amd64.S ([#5409](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5409)), always-emit compilation unit DIE ([#5412](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5412)), `--fdebug-prefix-path` assembler flag support ([#5418](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5418)), function sections with DWARF ([#4934](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4934)), and example with captured variables debug info ([#5422](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5422)) by [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon) and [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan)

- **chamelon minimizer** saw three merged PRs this week: module-based minimization representation ([#5356](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5356)), subcommands ([#5421](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5421)), and build system fix ([#5401](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5401)) by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp)

- **Mode hint system** applied to modules for better error messages when mode checking fails ([#5034](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5034)), and functor mode tests updated to use locality/visibility/statefulness axes ([#5311](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5311)) by [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) and [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js)

- **IRC register allocator** now has bit matrix CI coverage; the `BIT_MATRIX_THRESHOLD` regalloc parameter is now tested in the CI pipeline ([#5414](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5414)) by [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc)

- **`ocamlfilt` demangler** in pure OCaml continues review ([#5100](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5100)) by [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) and [@shym](https://github.com/shym), with discussion about whether to keep or drop the code pointer suffix in demangled names

- **Signal handler interop** PR ([#5044](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5044)) by [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) for `Signal_external` type addition remains open, with a discussion on signal chaining as an alternative approach

- **Dead exception handler bug** reported as issue [#5408](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5408) by [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls): the Flambda2 Simplify pass fails to remove exception handlers (and associated traps) when the local exception optimisation triggers
repositories.discussions_summary: Discussions centered on fiber cache correctness, dynlink ABI design, GC prefetch semantics, and performance trade-offs for the Sort.set interface change.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **Fiber cache correctness**: [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic) identified subtle liveness issues in the cross-domain fiber return implementation in [#5363](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5363), including a potential infinite loop in the cache-disabling path; [Max Slater](https://github.com/TheNumbat) iterated through multiple fixes

- **GC prefetch semantics**: [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan) in [#5416](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5416) explains that `prefetchw` takes an exclusive cache-line lock, serializing domains that share objects, while `prefetchr` avoids this; a benchmark showed >2× slowdown with `prefetchw` in multi-domain shared-object scenarios

- **dynlink ABI trade-offs**: In [#5420](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5420), [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) describes a subtle approach where `dynu_defines` header entries are mangled symbol strings treated as part of an ABI, allowing natdynlink to avoid depending on `Symbol.t` while preserving `ocamlobjinfo` compatibility

- **`Sort.set` interface vs. allocation**: [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin) in [#5419](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5419) measured that the `Option.iter` call in the old interface is actually inlined (no closure allocation), making the new `t`-taking interface slightly more allocating rather than less; the thread discusses whether simplicity outweighs the small regression

- **Preemption continuation sizing**: [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan) and [Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark) in [#4881](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4881) discuss the GC root scanning implications of preemptions at non-call GC points, including how continuation objects must be dynamically sized to store `gc_regs` and when those registers should be tagged
repositories.trends_summary: Development shows sustained focus on arm64 parity with x86, systematic CFG backend correctness hardening, and active chamelon tooling investment.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **arm64 feature parity** advancing rapidly: vec256 via tuple translation ([#5291](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5291)), more test enablement ([#5385](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5385)), boxed vector tag unification ([#5410](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5410)), and CFG invariant CI coverage for arm64 ([#5411](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5411)) reflect coordinated effort to close the gap with the x86 backend

- **CFG backend correctness** is receiving systematic attention from [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc) across five merged PRs this week ([#5296](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5296), [#5375](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5375), [#5182](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5182), [#5388](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5388), [#5390](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5390)); the pattern of adding invariant checks before adding features reduces the bug surface

- **chamelon tooling investment** with three consecutive PRs (module minimization, subcommands, dune integration) suggests it is maturing into a primary debugging workflow for the team ([#5356](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5356), [#5421](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5421), [#5423](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5423))

- **Flambda2 middle-end testability** improving through fexpr-based testsuite integration ([#5413](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5413)) and `-dfexpr-annot` ([#5210](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5210)), making it easier to write regression tests for optimizer passes

- **Small integer type ecosystem** is growing: indexing primitives ([#4779](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4779)), bit-count operations ([#5393](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5393)), and x86 intrinsics ([#5407](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5407)) collectively build out `int8`/`int16` unboxed type support toward parity with `int64`/`float`
repositories.contributors_summary: Sixteen contributors were active, spanning backend, runtime, type system, tooling, and build infrastructure work.

repositories.brief_summary: Signature-help improvements and verbose kind hover fixes landed, with ongoing signature-help work open for review.
repositories.new_features_summary: Signature help no longer loops past last parameter; hover now shows <default> for all-legacy modes; verbose kind display fixed.
repositories.new_features:
- **Signature help stops after the last parameter** — the handler now returns `None` when `active_param` is `None`, preventing the overlay from appearing after all arguments are consumed ([#214](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/214), [68a820f](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/commit/68a820fdab67900864cfc2263d3bea7a8df8aeb1))
- **Hover shows `<default>` instead of an empty string** when all mode annotations are legacy, making the output explicit rather than blank ([#219](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/219), [d5dfd8a](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/commit/d5dfd8abdf1c6e37c1e8c86b0f5888b1f706beb1))
