- **Verbose kind enclosing output corrected** — cherry-picked compiler changes from [oxcaml/oxcaml#5398](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5398) fix the modality list shown at higher verbosity levels (e.g. verbosity 2 now correctly lists `forkable`, `unyielding`, `portable`, `contended`, `static`, etc.) ([#218](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/218), [468e4b8](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/commit/468e4b81194eba4bbb5f5449b532e072e465b7e9))
- **`least_modalities` API replaces `least_modalities_implying`** in `typemode.mli`, gaining an `~include_implied` parameter that controls whether modalities implied by other written modalities are included ([#218](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/218))
repositories.activity_summary: Three open PRs await review covering signature-help with labelled args, optional arg suggestions, and unfinished let-bindings.
repositories.activity:
- **Ongoing: signature help with only labelled arguments** — [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) opened [#205](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/205) to add tests covering functions with exclusively labelled parameters; [Kostia Balytskyi](https://github.com/ikostia) requested an additional mixed positional/named-arg test case, and the author noted a dependency on upstream [ocaml/merlin#2032](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2032) before it can be completed
- **Ongoing: signature help should suggest optional arguments** — [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) opened [#216](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/216) and subsequently marked it as a draft pending upstream work on [ocaml/merlin#2032](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2032)
- **Ongoing: signature help on unfinished `let ... in` bindings** — [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) opened [#215](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/215) downstreaming [ocaml/merlin#2009](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2009); a fix is still under investigation
- **Merged: signature help no longer loops** — [#214](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/214) by [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) was merged by [Kostia Balytskyi](https://github.com/ikostia) after confirming the Flambda bot comment was not applicable to this change
- **Merged: hover output for all-legacy modes** — [#219](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/219) by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) merged despite transient CI failures traced to an opam-side issue affecting the main branch
- **Merged: compiler changes from oxcaml/oxcaml#5398** — [#218](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/218) by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) cherry-picked to unblock verbose kind hover fixes without waiting for the next scheduled compiler roll
repositories.discussions_summary: Review discussion on #218 included a detailed 4-way diff comparing oxcaml/oxcaml and merlin changes across shared typing files.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **4-way diff review on compiler cherry-pick** — [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) provided a thorough side-by-side comparison of the changes in `typing/printtyp.ml`, `typing/typemode.ml`, and `typing/typemode.mli` between [oxcaml/oxcaml#5398](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5398) and [#218](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/218), noting minor spacing differences; [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) addressed the feedback ([#218](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/218))
- **Upstream dependency coordination for signature help** — discussion on [#205](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/205) and [#216](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/216) surfaced the pattern that oxcaml-specific fixes are being held until [ocaml/merlin#2032](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2032) lands upstream
repositories.trends_summary: Signature-help reliability is the dominant theme, with multiple downstream PRs tracking upstream ocaml/merlin work.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Signature-help as a sustained focus** — four separate PRs this week ([#205](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/205), [#214](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/214), [#215](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/215), [#216](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/216)) address correctness edge-cases in signature help (looping, optional args, labelled args, unfinished bindings), indicating a systematic effort to bring the feature to parity
- **Eager compiler cherry-picks** — [#218](https://github.comml/oxcaml/merlin/issues/218) was explicitly cherry-picked ahead of the normal compiler roll cycle to deliver user-visible fixes sooner, suggesting a pattern of targeted backports rather than waiting for bulk rolls
- **Upstream/downstream coordination** — contributors are explicitly tracking [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) PRs before landing oxcaml-side changes, reflecting a deliberate policy of keeping divergence minimal
repositories.contributors_summary: Liam Stevenson merged two PRs (compiler cherry-pick and hover output fix); Charlène_Gros drove all signature-help work; Kostia Balytskyi reviewed and merged PRs and provided code-style feedback.
repositories.contributors:
- [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) authored and merged [#218](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/218) (compiler cherry-pick for verbose kind hover) and [#219](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/219) (hover output for all-legacy modes), and reviewed [#214](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/214)
- [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) authored [#205](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/205), [#214](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/214), [#215](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/215), and [#216](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/216), covering the full range of signature-help correctness work this week
- [Kostia Balytskyi](https://github.com/ikostia) merged [#214](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/214), reviewed [#205](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/205) requesting an additional test case, and provided code-style suggestions during the review of [#214](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/214)
- [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) reviewed [#218](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/218) with a detailed 4-way diff comparing the upstream and merlin-side changes across three shared typing files

repositories.brief_summary: js_of_ocaml package switched to track the upstream oxcaml branch directly, removing 47 individual patches.
repositories.new_features_summary: js_of_ocaml-compiler.6.0.1+ox now points to the oxcaml branch of js_of_ocaml upstream, eliminating a large local patch stack.
repositories.new_features:
- The `js_of_ocaml-compiler.6.0.1+ox` and `js_of_ocaml-ppx.6.0.1+ox` opam packages now source from the **oxcaml branch** of the upstream [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) repository (commit `c3da0bb`) rather than an older snapshot with local patches applied, enabling consumers to pick up OxCaml-specific fixes including parameterised library support needed for incremental jsoo compilation in dune ([ocaml/dune#13414](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13414)) ([#31](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/31))
- 47 individual patch files (covering atomics, float32, unboxed indexing, labeled tuples, mixed blocks, and more) were **removed from the patch stack** as their changes are now present directly in the upstream oxcaml branch, simplifying long-term maintenance ([#31](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/31))
repositories.activity_summary: PR #31, open since January 22, was merged, updating jsoo packages to track the oxcaml upstream branch directly.
repositories.activity: - [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) opened [#31](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/31) on January 22 and it was merged this week, updating `js_of_ocaml-compiler.6.0.1+ox` and related packages to point to the **oxcaml branch** of js_of_ocaml rather than applying a growing set of patches on top of an older snapshot; the PR description notes the branch has accumulated too many changes to ship as individual patches and that pointing to the public branch is easier for the community to maintain
repositories.discussions_summary: PR #31 included a note cc'ing Jérôme Vouillon as the jsoo maintainer about the oxcaml branch approach.
repositories.notable_discussions: - [#31](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/31) cc'd [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon), the upstream js_of_ocaml maintainer, as stakeholder in the decision to point directly to the public oxcaml branch rather than continuing to maintain a local patch series
repositories.trends_summary: Shift toward consuming upstream oxcaml-specific branches directly rather than maintaining local patches in this opam overlay.
repositories.emerging_trends: - The merge of [#31](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/31) continues a pattern of **reducing the local patch burden** in this OxCaml opam overlay by moving OxCaml-specific changes upstream and then consuming them via branch or commit pins rather than carrying patches
repositories.contributors_summary: [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) authored the PR and [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) merged it.
repositories.contributors:
- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) authored [#31](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/31), proposing and implementing the switch of `js_of_ocaml-compiler.6.0.1+ox` to track the upstream oxcaml branch and removing the accumulated local patch stack of 47 patches
- [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) merged [#31](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/31)

repositories.brief_summary: Eio 0.14+ compatibility update migrates meio from the deprecated Ctf API to the new Eio_runtime_events module.
repositories.new_features_summary: Migrated to Eio_runtime_events API and switched from nottui to nottui-unix for the UI loop.
repositories.new_features:
- **Migration from `Eio.Private.Ctf` to `Eio_runtime_events`**: the internal event callback system was rewritten to use the new `Eio_runtime_events` module and its typed `event` variant, replacing the older `Ctf`-based callback approach with a unified `RE.add_callbacks` call ([#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72), [673fdbd](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/commit/673fdbd110a5695b1ed297d938e1a27233464c68))
- **Switch from `nottui` to `nottui-unix`**: the UI event loop now uses `Nottui_unix.run` instead of `Nottui.Ui_loop.run`, reflecting an upstream split of the nottui library ([#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72), [673fdbd](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/commit/673fdbd110a5695b1ed297d938e1a27233464c68))
- **New `eio_name` helper library for examples**: a small `Eio_name` library was extracted into `example/eio_name.ml`, providing a `name` function that sets fiber names via `Eio.Private.Trace.name`, replacing direct use of `Eio.Private.Ctf.set_name` and `Eio.Private.Ctf.log` in the example programs ([#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72), [673fdbd](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/commit/673fdbd110a5695b1ed297d938e1a27233464c68))
- **`opam` dependency updates**: `meio.opam` now requires `eio >= 0.14`, `nottui-unix` (replacing `nottui`), and `dune >= 3.10`; the custom git pin-depends on a private eio fork have been removed, enabling installation from the standard opam repository ([#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72), [673fdbd](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/commit/673fdbd110a5695b1ed297d938e1a27233464c68))
- **Improved task kind display**: `console.ml` now renders task kinds using `Eio_runtime_events.pp_event` for unknown event types, and correctly distinguishes cancellation contexts (`cc`) from fibers (`task`) using the new variant patterns from `Eio_runtime_events.event` ([#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72), [673fdbd](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/commit/673fdbd110a5695b1ed297d938e1a27233464c68))
- **Removed `Ctf.with_tracing` requirement from examples**: the `burn.ml`, `burn_domains.ml`, `main.ml`, and `zero.ml` examples no longer need to wrap execution in `Ctf.with_tracing`, simplifying the example entry points ([#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72), [673fdbd](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/commit/673fdbd110a5695b1ed297d938e1a27233464c68))
- **Updated CI to OCaml 5.3 and `setup-ocaml@v3`**: the CI workflow was updated to target OCaml 5.3 (from 5.1.1) and uses the latest `ocaml/setup-ocaml@v3` action, removing the custom opam repository overrides and the custom notty pin ([#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72), [673fdbd](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/commit/673fdbd110a5695b1ed297d938e1a27233464c68))
repositories.activity_summary: PR #72 merging the Eio 0.14+ compatibility work, and issue #73 tracking the disabled screencast CI generation.
repositories.activity:
- [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) merged [#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72) "Eio.0.14+", a **rebased version of [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris)'s earlier work in [#71](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/71)**, bringing the codebase up to date with the modern Eio API surface
- [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) opened [#73](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/73) "Re-enable the screencast generation in CI", noting that the screencast pipeline depended on a patched version of the `notty` library that hardcoded terminal size; the fix requires either updating the workaround or adding terminal-size configuration upstream — the screencast steps are currently **commented out in CI** pending a solution
repositories.discussions_summary: Patrick Ferris acknowledged the Eio 0.14+ PR merge with a brief thank-you comment.
repositories.notable_discussions: - [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) commented on [#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72) thanking [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) for rebasing and landing the Eio 0.14+ compatibility work originally authored in [#71](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/71)
repositories.trends_summary: The project is modernising its Eio dependency and CI infrastructure following upstream Eio and notty library evolution.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Eio API modernisation**: the removal of `Eio.Private.Ctf` usage in favour of `Eio_runtime_events` and `Eio.Private.Trace` reflects the stabilisation of the Eio runtime-events interface in Eio 0.14+; meio is tracking these upstream changes to avoid relying on internal private APIs ([#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72))
- **CI infrastructure debt**: the screencast generation pipeline was disabled rather than updated ([#73](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/73)), pointing to a known maintenance gap around terminal-size handling in the `notty` dependency that will need resolution before animated documentation can be regenerated
repositories.contributors_summary: Tim McGilchrist drove the Eio 0.14+ update; Patrick Ferris's prior work in #71 formed the basis of the merged changes.
repositories.contributors:
- [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) rebased and merged [#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72) (Eio 0.14+ compatibility, 22 files changed), opened issue [#73](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/73) tracking the screencast CI regression, and landed the CI update to OCaml 5.3 and `setup-ocaml@v3`
- [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) authored the original Eio 0.14+ compatibility work in [#71](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/71) that was rebased into [#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72), and commented on the merged PR acknowledging the contribution

group_summaries.brief_summary: Major package releases, large-scale opam-repository archival, and extensive ocaml.org documentation quality improvements.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: New packages mkernel, utcp, virtfs, yocaml 3.0.0; scraper structured reporting; dune 3.21.1 and menhir 20260209 fixes.
group_summaries.new_features:
**ocaml/opam-repository**

- **mkernel 0.0.1** released by [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) — initial public release of a unikernel with Miou providing I/O for Solo5/Unikraft targets ([ocaml/opam-repository#29361](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29361))
- **utcp 0.0.1** released by [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) — initial public release of a pure OCaml TCP implementation ([ocaml/opam-repository#29359](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29359))
- **virtfs 1.0.0** released by [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) — first release exposing `Path`, `Tree`, and `Tree.Simple` modules for abstract file path and file tree representation ([ocaml/opam-repository#29381](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29381))
- **yocaml 3.0.0** (14 packages) released by [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) — major version of the YOCaml static site generator switching from `int` to `float` for time/mtime, adding `Action.write_files`, `Data.Validation.req`/`opt`, and improved validation error pretty-printing ([ocaml/opam-repository#29377](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29377))
- **dune 3.21.1** (17 packages) released by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) — patch release fixing build issues on NetBSD and OpenBSD, fixing `melange.emit` package mask behavior, and stopping RPC server from starting on `dune promote` ([ocaml/opam-repository#29374](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29374))
- **menhir 20260209** (5 packages) released by [François Pottier](https://github.com/fpottier) — snapshot release fixing Windows installation issues caused by `aux.ml` filename in the prior 20260203 release ([ocaml/opam-repository#29364](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29364), [ocaml/opam-repository#29365](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29365))
- **mirage-crypto 2.0.3** (7 packages) released by [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) — adds `mirage-crypto-rng-mkernel` and PPC macros support ([ocaml/opam-repository#29368](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29368))
- **trace 0.11** (4 packages) and **tracy-client 0.7** released by [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) — trace 0.11 is a major rework of the collector using an open sum type for spans, removing global state, and adding extensible metrics; tracy-client 0.7 updates to Tracy v0.13.1 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29345](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29345), [ocaml/opam-repository#29346](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29346))
- **grace 0.3.0** released by [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob) — diagnostics library removing `base`/`core` dependency, replacing `Source.reader` with `Source.Reader.t`, adding line breaks in large diagnostics and TTY detection ([ocaml/opam-repository#29379](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29379))
- **containers 3.18** (2 packages) released by [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) — breaking changes to `CCListLabel`, `CCFloat.{min,max}` semantics aligned with Stdlib for NaN, and `CCInt.rem` negative number behavior ([ocaml/opam-repository#29372](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29372))
- **miou 0.5.3** and **miou 0.5.4** released by [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) — 0.5.3 fixes signal handling and transfer to dom0; 0.5.4 fixes macOS support and the return value of `poll(3P)` ([ocaml/opam-repository#29378](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29378), [ocaml/opam-repository#29400](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29400))
- **ocp-index 1.4.1** (2 packages) released by [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) — adds support for OCaml 5.5 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29358](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29358))
- **grenier 0.16** released by [Frédéric Bour](https://github.com/let-def) — adds `is_singleton` for OCaml 5.5 compatibility and rank-based element access functions ([ocaml/opam-repository#29349](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29349))
- **camlzip 1.14** released by [Xavier Leroy](https://github.com/xavierleroy) — fixes local file header errors for files > 4 GB ([ocaml/opam-repository#29352](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29352))
- **symex 0.1** released by [@redianthus](https://github.com/redianthus) — initial release of symbolic execution library ([ocaml/opam-repository#29356](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29356))
- **smtml 0.21.0** released by [@filipeom](https://github.com/filipeom) — SMT solver frontend with type-safe API for uninterpreted functions ([ocaml/opam-repository#29357](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29357))
- **multipart_form 0.8.0** (5 packages) released by [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) — fixes eio support and updates to ocamlformat 0.28.1 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29367](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29367))
- **lintcstubs-arity 0.5.1** released by [Török Edwin](https://github.com/edwintorok) — adds OCaml >= 5.2 support for the C binding header generator ([ocaml/opam-repository#29371](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29371))
- **passage 0.3.3** released by [José Nogueira](https://github.com/thatportugueseguy) — restores macOS availability ([ocaml/opam-repository#29383](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29383))
- **oktree 0.2.2** released by [Paul Garner](https://github.com/anentropic) — simple octree implementation for nearest-neighbor search in 3D space ([ocaml/opam-repository#29376](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29376))
- **spectrum 0.7.0** released by [Paul Garner](https://github.com/anentropic) — terminal color and formatting library using OCaml Format module semantic tags ([ocaml/opam-repository#29382](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29382))
- **bogue 20260208** released by [珊瑚](https://github.com/sanette) — new version of the SDL2-based GUI library ([ocaml/opam-repository#29351](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29351))

**ocaml/ocaml.org**

- The **OCaml Planet scraper** gained a new `Scrape_report` module returning structured results (`New_post`, `Video_update`, `Error`) and CLI flags `--commit-file`/`--report-file` to generate descriptive PR bodies listing new posts and broken feeds ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3503](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3503))
- **Scheduled scraping workflows** now skip execution on repository forks via an `if: github.repository == 'ocaml/ocaml.org'` guard, preventing unnecessary CI failures on forks ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3508](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3508))
group_summaries.group_overview:
- **ocaml/opam-repository** saw 22+ new package releases merged, including significant releases of dune 3.21.1, menhir 20260209, yocaml 3.0.0, mirage-crypto 2.0.3, trace 0.11, and containers 3.18, along with two large archival PRs removing ~46,000 files of unmaintained packages ([ocaml/opam-repository#29360](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29360), [ocaml/opam-repository#29363](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29363))
- **ocaml/ocaml.org** focused on documentation quality: [Bozhidar Batsov](https://github.com/bbatsov) fixed ~100 typos across 41 tutorial files ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3491](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3491)) and resolved 469 markdownlint violations ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3492](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3492)), alongside Planet scraper infrastructure improvements ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3503](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3503))
- Combined contributor count: 23+ unique contributors across ocaml/opam-repository and 8+ across ocaml/ocaml.org
- Several incoming packages targeted new concurrency, networking, and formal verification domains: mkernel, utcp, symex, smtml ([ocaml/opam-repository#29361](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29361), [ocaml/opam-repository#29359](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29359), [ocaml/opam-repository#29356](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29356), [ocaml/opam-repository#29357](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29357))
- OCaml 5.5 compatibility work was visible across multiple packages (grenier 0.16, ocp-index 1.4.1), indicating preparation for the next OCaml release ([ocaml/opam-repository#29349](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29349), [ocaml/opam-repository#29358](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29358))
group_summaries.activity_summary: Ongoing work includes js_of_ocaml 6.3.2 integration, windtrap testing framework, ortools 9.15.0 C++ rewrite, i18n for ocaml.org, and archival policy discussion.
group_summaries.activity:
**ocaml/opam-repository**

- **js_of_ocaml 6.3.2** (8 packages) opened by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) to replace [ocaml/opam-repository#29388](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29388), fixing shell completion file installation and labeled "consider for merge" ([ocaml/opam-repository#29393](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29393)); the 6.3.0 PR ([ocaml/opam-repository#29348](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29348)) brought wasm number unboxing, reference unboxing, constant sinking, and JS-parser improvements but awaits resolution of compatibility issues
- **windtrap 0.1.0** new all-in-one OCaml testing framework opened by [Thibaut Mattio](https://github.com/tmattio) — unifies unit, property-based, snapshot, and expect tests under a single PPX-enabled API with CLI runner, filter, and bisect_ppx coverage integration ([ocaml/opam-repository#29387](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29387))
- **ortools 9.15.0** (2 packages) opened by [Timothy Bourke](https://github.com/tbrk) — Google OR-Tools OCaml interface rewritten in C++ to avoid unnecessary copies, accompanied by new virtual packages conf-bzip2 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29395](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29395)), conf-eigen3 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29396](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29396)), and conf-zlib updates ([ocaml/opam-repository#29394](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29394)) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29380](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29380))
- **Package archival** — [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) merged two archival PRs: `available: false` packages (594 files, [ocaml/opam-repository#29360](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29360)) and `x-maintenance-intent: none` packages primarily tezos/octez (45,238 files across 1,197 packages, [ocaml/opam-repository#29363](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29363)); the broader draft archival PR [ocaml/opam-repository#29058](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29058) was closed pending further policy discussion
- **Immutable policy discussion** — ongoing PR by [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) to tighten repository immutability rules, with [Marcello Seri](https://github.com/mseri) suggesting merge and handling pin-depends separately ([ocaml/opam-repository#29072](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29072))
- **Dependency compatibility fixes** merged: upper bound on containers 3.18 for `decoders` and `ppx_cstubs` by [Marcello Seri](https://github.com/mseri) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29391](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29391)), upper bound `lwt < 6.1.0` for `timmy-lwt` ([ocaml/opam-repository#29390](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29390)), and upper bounds on trace revdeps including `fuseau` ([ocaml/opam-repository#29347](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29347), [ocaml/opam-repository#29370](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29370))
- **opam custom-install compatibility** issue opened by [Alina Banerjee](https://github.com/alinab) — `opam-custom-install` fails with opam 2.1 against a 2.2 root, identified as needing `available: opam-version >= x & opam-version < y` constraints ([ocaml/opam-repository#29369](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29369))
- **frama-clang 0.0.19** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29309](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29309)) by [Basile Desloges](https://github.com/zilbuz) and [Virgile Prevosto](https://github.com/vprevosto) — ongoing work resolving conf-libclang distribution detection (opensuse vs. open-suse)

**ocaml/ocaml.org**

- **Emacs editor setup tutorial** update opened by [Bozhidar Batsov](https://github.com/bbatsov) ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3509](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3509)) streamlines mode recommendations toward Eglot + ocamllsp; under active review with [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac)
- **Pending cleanup PRs** by [Bozhidar Batsov](https://github.com/bbatsov): typo and lint fixes in root-level docs ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3493](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3493)), lint fixes across exercises including a filename correction ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3494](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3494)), and lint fixes across data directories ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3495](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3495)), all awaiting rebase after [ocaml/ocaml.org#3500](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3500)
- **Internationalization initiative** continues: two draft/open PRs by [mt_caret](https://github.com/mt-caret) — foundational i18n infrastructure with language frontmatter and selector ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3238](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3238)) and additional Japanese translations of getting-started pages ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3489](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3489))
- **Module documentation TOC** feature request by [Arthur Carcano](https://github.com/krtab) — proposes clickable table of contents for functions in module documentation pages, similar to docs.rs sidebar ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3506](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3506))
- **Neovim plugin instructions** update ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3421](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3421)) by [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) remains open with merge conflicts
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **dune 3.21.1** released to ocaml/opam-repository ([ocaml/opam-repository#29374](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29374)) affects the build system used by ocaml/ocaml.org itself; the patch fixes BSD build and RPC server behavior that could affect website build pipelines
- **OCaml 5.5 compatibility** packages merged to ocaml/opam-repository (ocp-index 1.4.1 at [ocaml/opam-repository#29358](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29358), grenier 0.16 at [ocaml/opam-repository#29349](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29349)) are dependencies of or relate to tooling used in the ocaml.org ecosystem, including the odoc documentation pipeline
- **Planet scraper feed quality** improved in ocaml/ocaml.org ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3497](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3497), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3503](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3503)) benefits the visibility of packages released to ocaml/opam-repository through blog announcements on Planet OCaml
- **[Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm)** actively participated in both repositories this week — merging PRs on ocaml/opam-repository and encouraging contributors to announce packages on discuss.ocaml.org, which feeds into ocaml.org Planet content
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **js_of_ocaml compatibility threads** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29348](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29348)) revealed that `timmy-lwt` breaks with `lwt` 6.1.0 and `data-encoding` tests fail due to NaN comparison using `=` instead of `compare`; [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) noted these failures are pre-existing, coordinating with [Marcello Seri](https://github.com/mseri) and [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) on the merge timeline
- **containers 3.18 breaking changes** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29372](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29372)) prompted discussion about downstream impact: `CCResult.result` removal causing `decoders` failures, and a stack overflow in `containers.pvec` tests under property-based testing which [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) fixed by addressing a size explosion in qcheck
- **Archival policy discussion** around [ocaml/opam-repository#29058](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29058) was closed, with [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) splitting the work into two less controversial PRs targeting `available: false` packages and packages with `x-maintenance-intent: none` (primarily tezos/octez), while the broader policy awaits further discussion
- **Agentic PR review workflow** discussion emerged in [ocaml/ocaml.org#3496](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3496): [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac) proposed requiring one narrow category of change per PR with a machine-verifiable check script; [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) and [Bozhidar Batsov](https://github.com/bbatsov) offered different perspectives on whether per-lint-rule granularity is necessary
- **Exercise page rendering** concern surfaced during the ocaml.org lint discussion ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3500](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3500)): [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) noted that ood-gen uses a regex `Str.regexp {|#[ ]*Solution|}` to split exercise markdown, and heading demotion could break this; [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac) verified `## Solution` still matches the unanchored regex
- **Translation sustainability** debated in [ocaml/ocaml.org#3238](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3238): [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) noted translated docs go stale quickly without a dedicated translation team; [mt_caret](https://github.com/mt-caret) proposed periodic LLM-assisted translation updates with a human Japanese reviewer
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Robur Coop / MirageOS ecosystem expansion**: [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) published three packages — `mkernel` ([ocaml/opam-repository#29361](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29361)), `miou` 0.5.3 and 0.5.4 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29378](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29378), [ocaml/opam-repository#29400](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29400)), and `multipart_form` 0.8.0 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29367](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29367)) in quick succession, reflecting active development of the MirageOS/Robur toolchain
- **OCaml 5.5 compatibility preparation**: multiple packages adding OCaml 5.5 support (grenier 0.16 at [ocaml/opam-repository#29349](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29349), ocp-index 1.4.1 at [ocaml/opam-repository#29358](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29358), lintcstubs-arity 0.5.1 at [ocaml/opam-repository#29371](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29371)) indicating coordinated preparation ahead of the next OCaml release
- **Breaking change coordination**: several releases included breaking API changes (containers 3.18 at [ocaml/opam-repository#29372](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29372), grace 0.3.0 at [ocaml/opam-repository#29379](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29379), trace 0.11 at [ocaml/opam-repository#29345](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29345)) requiring coordinated revdep upper bound additions by maintainers
- **AI-assisted contributions** are visibly increasing in ocaml/ocaml.org: the `agentic-pr` label was introduced by [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac) and applied to multiple PRs ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3500](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3500), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3503](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3503), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3508](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3508)), reflecting an emerging norm of tagging AI-generated work for appropriate review handling
- **Documentation quality tooling investment**: `markdownlint-cli2` applied across the full ocaml.org tutorial corpus, 469 violations resolved ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3492](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3492)), with `agentic-pr` workflow established — suggesting ongoing automation of content quality checks ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3491](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3491), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3492](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3492))
- **Large-scale repository maintenance**: the archival of ~46,000 files of unmaintained packages to [ocaml/opam-repository-archive](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository-archive) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29360](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29360), [ocaml/opam-repository#29363](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29363)) represents a concerted effort to keep the repository focused on actively-maintained software

group_summaries.brief_summary: Active week across dune, opam, ocaml-lsp, and js_of_ocaml with sandboxing expansions, LSP build-system decoupling, and two patch releases.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Dune adds sandboxing defaults, trace events, odoc-config generation; ocaml-lsp gains build-system-agnostic merlin config; js_of_ocaml ships CTZ-based BitSet and bounded Set APIs.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: New `--no-recursive` flag for `dune describe workspace` limits output to specified directories, useful for large monorepos ([ocaml/dune#13590](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13590))
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: Dune now emits trace events for direct file writes (install files, lock files), visible in `dune trace cat` output ([ocaml/dune#13618](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13618))
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: Melange `(library ..)` and `(melange.emit ..)` stanzas sandboxed by default from dune lang 3.22 ([ocaml/dune#13619](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13619))
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: All `(test)`, `(tests)`, and related stanza variants sandboxed by default from dune lang 3.22 ([ocaml/dune#13617](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13617))
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: Dune now writes and installs `odoc-config.sexp` files for packages declaring `:with-doc` dependencies, enabling cross-package documentation builds with odoc ([ocaml/dune#13476](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13476), closes [ocaml/dune#13485](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13485))
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: Fixed deadlock in the `memo` library when dependency cycles are detected ([ocaml/dune#13625](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13625))
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: Fixed sandboxed builds of `(library (stdlib (modules_before_stdlib ..)))` by including implicit dependency edges in ocamldep results ([ocaml/dune#13624](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13624))
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: Fixed `(dir ..)` field on package declarations being ignored when assigning cram tests to packages ([ocaml/dune#13581](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13581))
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: Fixed `dune trace cat --follow` exiting prematurely on subprocess action trace events ([ocaml/dune#13607](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13607))
- **[ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp)**: New env var `$OCAMLLSP_PROJECT_BUILD_SYSTEM` allows any build system to serve merlin configuration via `merlin-dot-protocol` ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1581](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1581))
- **[ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp)**: New `$OCAMLLSP_PROJECT_ROOT` env var explicitly specifies project root; `$OCAMLLSP_PROJECT_ROOT_MARKERS` enables automatic root detection by marker files ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1581](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1581))
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml)**: New `Set.compare_cardinal_with` provides O(min(n, k)) cardinality comparison with early-exit; new `Set.to_list_bounded` returns a sorted list only if the set has at most N elements ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2180](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2180))
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml)**: `BitSet.next_free` and `BitSet.next_mem` rewritten using count-trailing-zeros (CTZ) for word-at-a-time iteration, reducing overhead for sparse and dense bitsets ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2179](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2179))
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml)**: `Dgraph.Make` functor relaxed to accept custom `SET` and `MAP` modules instead of requiring the full standard `Set.S`/`Map.S` signatures ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2178](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2178))
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml)**: Shell completion files now correctly install under monorepo/workspace builds, fixing a dune regression ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2186](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2186))
- **[ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam)**: README configure instructions corrected to distinguish `./configure` from `./configure --with-vendored-deps` ([ocaml/opam#6858](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6858))
group_summaries.group_overview:
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)** was the most active repository with multiple merged PRs covering sandboxing expansion, trace subsystem fixes, package management improvements, and a fix for a memo deadlock ([ocaml/dune#13625](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13625)); 3.21.1 release preparation is underway ([ocaml/dune#13557](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13557))
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml)** shipped two patch releases (6.3.1 on 2026-02-13, 6.3.2 on 2026-02-15) to fix shell completion install regressions, alongside several compiler performance improvements
- **[ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp)** merged a month-long PR ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1581](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1581)) enabling build-system-agnostic merlin configuration via environment variables
- **[ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam)** saw continued review activity on two performance PRs ([ocaml/opam#6489](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6489), [ocaml/opam#6283](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6283)) and a new fix for PATH ordering in the env hook ([ocaml/opam#6859](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6859))
- **[ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc)** had one open PR ([ocaml/odoc#1400](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1400)) under review; **[ocaml/ocamlfind](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind)** closed a long-standing issue ([ocaml/ocamlfind#44](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/44)) about `Fl_dynload` support in Dune builds
- **[ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin)**, **[ocaml/opam-file-format](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-file-format)**, and **[ocaml-dune/spawn](https://github.com/ocaml-dune/spawn)** had no recorded activity this week
group_summaries.activity_summary: Dune 3.21.1 release prep, opam update performance PRs under review, js_of_ocaml compiler cleanup, and ocaml-lsp web-worker issue closure.
group_summaries.activity:
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)** 3.21.1 release preparation ([ocaml/dune#13557](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13557)): backport PRs ([ocaml/dune#13560](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13560), [ocaml/dune#13561](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13561)) were prepared; [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) opened [ocaml/dune#13627](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13627) to merge only changelog entries from the release branch rather than performing a full merge
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)** trace subsystem cluster: file-write events ([ocaml/dune#13618](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13618)), ordered at-exit hooks ([ocaml/dune#13608](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13608), [ocaml/dune#13609](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13609), [ocaml/dune#13605](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13605)), `--follow` exit detection fix ([ocaml/dune#13607](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13607)), and JSON action trace payload fix ([ocaml/dune#13604](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13604)) all merged
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)** rev store: [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) merged a fix preventing false cache hits ([ocaml/dune#13588](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13588)) and added a git test utilities module ([ocaml/dune#13593](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13593)); [Stephen Sherratt](https://github.com/gridbugs) opened [ocaml/dune#13592](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13592) to recognize default dune build commands in lockfiles
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)** js_of_ocaml incremental build: [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) opened three related PRs ([ocaml/dune#13611](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13611), [ocaml/dune#13612](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13612), [ocaml/dune#13613](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13613)) for demand-driven jsoo rule generation and per-unit JS compilation, plus standalone runtime sharing ([ocaml/dune#13621](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13621))
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)** test suite: cleanup PRs removing `--display short` flags ([ocaml/dune#13601](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13601), [ocaml/dune#13614](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13614)) and unnecessary temp dir settings ([ocaml/dune#13615](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13615)) merged by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)** binary distribution: [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) opened [ocaml/dune#13610](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13610) proposing to consolidate `dune-bin` and `binary-distribution` into a single install mechanism
- **[ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam)** depexts PR [ocaml/opam#6489](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6489) by [@arozovyk](https://github.com/arozovyk) received review from [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) and [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) covering function naming, Windows compatibility in sandbox checks, and test output cleanup (fixes [ocaml/opam#6461](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6461), [ocaml/opam#4172](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/4172))
- **[ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam)** `opam update` speedup draft PR [ocaml/opam#6283](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6283) by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) received a minor update, targeting ~8x speedup on subsequent empty updates (fixes [ocaml/opam#5553](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/5553))
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml)** compiler cleanup batch ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2178](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2178)) merged, covering Map.update adoption, find_opt usage, and module removal; open PRs for subst speedup ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2185](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2185)) and block sharing preservation ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2184](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2184)) remain under review
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml)** Wasm-related long-running PRs remain open: Wasm fixes ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2154](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2154)), Bigstring optimizations ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2144](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2144)), WASI runtime ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831)), Stack Switching effects ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#1832](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1832)), all by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon)
- **[ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp)** issue [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#857](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/857), a 2022 feature request from [Emilio Jesús Gallego Arias](https://github.com/ejgallego) on web-worker support, was closed on February 11
- **[ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc)** PR [ocaml/odoc#1400](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1400) by [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam), fixing bug [ocaml/odoc#930](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/930) with 522 lines added across 24 files, was updated February 9 and awaits review
- **[ocaml/ocamlfind](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind)** closed issue [ocaml/ocamlfind#44](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/44) (originally filed in May 2022 by [Emilio Jesús Gallego Arias](https://github.com/ejgallego)) regarding improved build-time `Fl_dynload` support for Coq plugins in Dune builds
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **dune ↔ odoc integration**: [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) merged [ocaml/dune#13476](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13476), which generates and installs `odoc-config.sexp` files for packages with `:with-doc` dependencies; this directly enables the cross-package documentation workflow that [ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc) targets (closes [ocaml/dune#13485](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13485))
- **dune ↔ js_of_ocaml build integration**: [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) opened three dune-side PRs ([ocaml/dune#13611](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13611), [ocaml/dune#13612](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13612), [ocaml/dune#13613](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13613)) to make jsoo rule generation demand-driven; concurrently he maintains the OXCaml support PR ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2105](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2105)) on the js_of_ocaml side, reflecting coordinated work across both repos
- **js_of_ocaml monorepo build regression triggered by dune**: the shell completion install bug in js_of_ocaml was traced to [ocaml/dune#13500](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13500) (dune's handling of generated files in monorepos), reported by [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) and fixed via [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2186](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2186) on the js_of_ocaml side, necessitating two patch releases
- **ocaml-lsp ↔ merlin config protocol**: the merged [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1581](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1581) uses `merlin-dot-protocol` to extend the merlin configuration surface to arbitrary build systems, bridging [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin)'s config protocol with [ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp)'s build system interface
- **opam ↔ dune dependency coordination**: [Stephen Sherratt](https://github.com/gridbugs) was active in both [ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam) (PATH env hook [ocaml/opam#6859](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6859) and README fix [ocaml/opam#6858](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6858)) and [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) (in-process lockfile build detection [ocaml/dune#13592](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13592)), reflecting ongoing coordination between the two package management toolchains
- **ocamlfind ↔ dune findlib resolution**: the closed [ocaml/ocamlfind#44](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/44) was originally filed because ocamlfind could not resolve package paths from `_build/default/META` files produced by dune, highlighting the ongoing dependency between ocamlfind and dune's build layout
- **OCaml upstream alert propagation in dune** ([ocaml/dune#13622](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13622)): `[@@@alert ...]` attributes are not propagated through dune's alias modules in wrapped libraries; [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) confirmed this is a known OCaml compiler bug ([ocaml/ocaml#11867](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11867)) with no dune-side fix currently possible
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **dune 3.21.1 release branch merge strategy** ([ocaml/dune#13557](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13557)): [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder), [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter), and [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) discussed whether to merge the release branch back into main; the team settled on merging only the changelog entries via [ocaml/dune#13627](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13627), avoiding merge conflicts and preserving clean history for the patch release tag
- **Scope of dune-build detection in lockfiles** ([ocaml/dune#13592](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13592)): [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) raised concerns that exact-match detection of dune-generated build commands is too fragile — packages omitting `@doc`, adding `-j1`, or calling dune differently would not be recognized; [Stephen Sherratt](https://github.com/gridbugs) discussed whether dune should instead annotate generated opam files with a metadata field
- **OCaml alerts in wrapped libraries** ([ocaml/dune#13622](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13622)): a user reported `[@@@alert ...]` attributes on modules are not propagated through dune's generated alias modules; [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) confirmed this is a known upstream OCaml bug ([ocaml/ocaml#11867](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11867)) with no dune-side workaround currently feasible
- **opam switch link silently breaking an existing `_opam` switch** ([ocaml/opam#6857](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6857)): [Matthieu Sozeau](https://github.com/mattam82) reported that running `opam switch link` on an already-linked switch directory silently corrupts the switch; [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) cross-referenced [ocaml/opam#6449](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6449) and requested version confirmation
- **Installing a package from a standalone opam file** ([ocaml/opam#4227](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/4227)): [Pierre Villemot](https://github.com/Halbaroth) added support for the long-standing feature request to install a package directly from a standalone opam file with a `url` field, citing a need to simplify pre-submission testing workflows
- **js_of_ocaml variable coalescing assertion failure** ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2166](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2166)): [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) reported an assertion failure in `js_variable_coalescing.ml` while rebasing, which [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) was unable to reproduce, leaving the issue unresolved
- **OXCaml Null representation compatibility in js_of_ocaml** ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2105](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2105)): [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) raised a compatibility question about OXCaml's `Null` representation versus the existing Null in [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#949](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/949); [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) explained the js_of_ocaml vs wasm_of_ocaml difference and noted the Wasm-side change would be straightforward
- **Monorepo build breakage from completion file install** ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2181](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2181)): [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) reported workspace build failure referencing [ocaml/dune#13500](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13500), prompting two subsequent patch releases of js_of_ocaml
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Sandboxing as the default**: dune is progressively enabling sandboxing by default for new language versions — test stanzas ([ocaml/dune#13617](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13617)) and Melange rules ([ocaml/dune#13619](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13619)) both moved to sandbox-by-default in 3.22, continuing a pattern established in earlier dune releases
- **Build observability investment in dune**: the `dune trace` subsystem received multiple fixes and a new file-write event type this week ([ocaml/dune#13618](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13618), [ocaml/dune#13607](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13607), [ocaml/dune#13604](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13604), [ocaml/dune#13605](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13605), [ocaml/dune#13608](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13608)), indicating ongoing investment in build performance analysis tooling
- **js_of_ocaml incrementality in dune**: three coordinated PRs from [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) ([ocaml/dune#13611](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13611), [ocaml/dune#13612](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13612), [ocaml/dune#13613](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13613)) aim to make jsoo builds demand-driven and per-compilation-unit, reducing redundant build artifacts ([ocaml/dune#13621](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13621))
- **Compiler micro-optimizations in js_of_ocaml**: a sustained pass to reduce compiler overhead landed this week — BitSet CTZ iteration ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2179](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2179)), Set.cardinal early-exit ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2180](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2180)), Set shortcircuit ops ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2182](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2182)), with more pending ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2185](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2185))
- **Decoupling ocaml-lsp from dune**: the merged [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1581](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1581) extends LSP support to non-dune build systems via `merlin-dot-protocol`, reflecting a broader effort to make the OCaml toolchain more build-system-agnostic
- **opam update and environment correctness**: two open PRs ([ocaml/opam#6283](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6283), [ocaml/opam#6489](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6489)) and the new [ocaml/opam#6859](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6859) reflect a sustained effort to improve update performance (HTTP caching, depext staleness detection) and shell environment correctness (PATH ordering)
- **In-process dependency builds in dune**: [ocaml/dune#13592](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13592) represents early exploration of building opam packages within the same dune process rather than shelling out, which could reduce overhead for pure-dune dependency trees
- **Wasm backend expansion in js_of_ocaml**: multiple long-running draft PRs (WASI runtime [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831), Stack Switching effects [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#1832](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1832), Bigstring optimizations [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2144](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2144), OXCaml support [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2105](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2105)) reflect sustained multi-month effort on broadening the Wasm target's capabilities

group_summaries.brief_summary: 34 PRs merged in oxcaml/oxcaml spanning SIMD, abstract kinds, chamelon tooling, runtime fixes, and arm64 parity; jsoo patch stack eliminated.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Abstract kinds, small-int SIMD intrinsics, chamelon subcommands/dune integration, manual module init, vec256 on arm64, and fexpr test infrastructure.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **Abstract kinds (part 2)** merges basic abstract kinds and kind aliases into the type system; environment arguments are now threaded through jkind operations ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5071](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5071))
- **Indexing and arraylike primitives for small integers** adds `%caml_bytes_geti8`, `%caml_bytes_geti16`, and related indexed-by primitives supporting `int8` and `int16#` indices for arrays, strings, bigstrings, and bytes ([oxcaml/oxcaml#4779](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4779))
- **`int16` x86 intrinsics** — `caml_popcnt_int16`, `caml_lzcnt_int16`, and `caml_bmi_tzcnt_int16` added to the SIMD backend; simdgen extended to support instructions with multiple legacy prefixes ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5407](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5407))
- **Small int `ctz`, `clz`, `popcnt`** primitives added for `int8` and `int16` unboxed types ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5393](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5393))
- **`[%%expect_asm]` support** added to expect tests, enabling verification of generated x86_64 assembly with architecture-tagged expectations ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5350](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5350))
- **`chamelon run` subcommand** introduced as the new canonical interface; old `chamelon -c '...' file.ml` syntax shows a deprecation warning ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5421](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5421))
- **`chamelon dune` subcommand** plus `minimize-failure` and `minimize-stdlib` Makefile targets; parses dune output to automatically minimize compilation failures ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5423](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5423))
- **Manual module initialization** via new `-manual-module-init` linker flag; `caml_init_module`, `caml_init_module_exn`, and `caml_init_module_from_ocaml` allow on-demand initialization with dependency tracking ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5395](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5395))
- **`-dfexpr-annot` compiler flag** dumps flambda2 fexpr representations alongside `.cmx` files at multiple pipeline stages ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5210](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5210))
- **`let poly_` and `val poly_` syntax** parsing support added for layout-polymorphic bindings ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5392](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5392))
- **`runtime5` added to `ocamlc -config` output** so downstream tooling can detect the runtime variant ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5415](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5415))
- **Wide vectors (vec256) translated to pairs on arm64** using two `vec128` operations in Lambda IR, enabling most 256-bit SIMD tests on arm without AVX ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5291](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5291))
- **Bit matrix for IRC interference graph** selectable via `-regalloc-param BIT_MATRIX_THRESHOLD:k`; `O(n²/8)` memory with better cache locality ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5296](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5296))
- **GC prefetch fix** — `prefetchr` replaces `prefetchw` in the major GC, fixing exclusive cache-line locking that made multi-domain programs over 2× slower when sharing objects ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5416](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5416))
- **Boxed vectors use tag 0** on amd64, matching arm64 representation and removing `Abstract_tag` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5410](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5410))
- **Fiber caches return fibers to the allocating domain** by tracking `domain_idx` in `stack_info` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5363](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5363))
- **fexpr testsuite actions** added via ocamltest integration (`dump-raw-fexpr`, `dump-simplify-fexpr`, `dump-reaper-fexpr`, `check-fexpr-dump`, `run-fexpr`) for middle-end testing ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5413](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5413))
- **`configure` checks required tool versions** with clear error messages for wrong OCaml, dune, or menhir versions ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5348](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5348))
- **`oxcaml-dev` opam package** simplifies development switch setup to three `opam` commands ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5344](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5344))
- **js_of_ocaml opam packages** (`js_of_ocaml-compiler.6.0.1+ox`, `js_of_ocaml-ppx.6.0.1+ox`) now source from the oxcaml branch of the upstream repository, eliminating 47 local patches ([oxcaml/opam-repository#31](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/31))
- **Signature help stops after the last parameter** in oxcaml/merlin, preventing the overlay appearing after all arguments are consumed ([oxcaml/merlin#214](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/214))
- **Hover shows `<default>` for all-legacy modes** in oxcaml/merlin rather than an empty string ([oxcaml/merlin#219](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/219))
- **Verbose kind enclosing output corrected** in oxcaml/merlin via cherry-picked compiler changes from [oxcaml/oxcaml#5398](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5398) ([oxcaml/merlin#218](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/218))
group_summaries.group_overview:
- **oxcaml/oxcaml** had 34 merged PRs across backend (SIMD, arm64, CFG), runtime (fibers, GC, prefetch), type system (abstract kinds, modes, modalities), tooling (chamelon, fexpr), and build infrastructure
- **oxcaml/opam-repository** merged one significant PR ([oxcaml/opam-repository#31](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/31)) eliminating 47 local patches for js_of_ocaml by switching to the upstream oxcaml branch
- **oxcaml/merlin** merged three PRs ([oxcaml/merlin#214](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/214), [oxcaml/merlin#218](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/218), [oxcaml/merlin#219](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/219)) addressing signature-help and hover output correctness
- **oxcaml/js_of_ocaml** and **janestreet/opam-repository** had no recorded activity this week
- Sixteen contributors were active across oxcaml/oxcaml alone; coordinated work spans compiler, tooling, and opam layers
group_summaries.activity_summary: CFG backend correctness hardening, upstream OCaml 5.4 merge progress, DWARF/debugging infrastructure, preemption work, and merlin signature-help improvements.
group_summaries.activity:
- **CFG backend invariants** expanded by [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc) across five merged PRs: terminator arity checks ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5388](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5388)), basic instruction arity checks ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5390](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5390)), liveness-at-entry checks ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5375](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5375)), destruction-point synchronization ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5182](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5182)), and a new arm64 CI job ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5411](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5411))
- **Upstream OCaml 5.4 merge** in progress via large draft PR ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5325](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5325)); build system conflicts resolved ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5376](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5376)), missing dynlink prerequisites merged ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5403](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5403)), and dynlink emancipation backport open for review ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5420](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5420))
- **DWARF/debugging** work has multiple open PRs from [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon): CFI information tests ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5409](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5409)), always-emit compilation unit DIE ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5412](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5412)), `--fdebug-prefix-path` support ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5418](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5418)), function sections with DWARF ([oxcaml/oxcaml#4934](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4934)), and captured variable debug info example ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5422](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5422))
- **Preemption work** by [Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark): initial preemption implementation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#4881](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4881)) and tick thread refactoring ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5349](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5349)) ongoing; race fix for `requested_external_interrupt` initialization merged ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5424](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5424))
- **Mode/modality type system** work: functors affected by modalities ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5371](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5371)), modules must be `aliased many` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5364](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5364)), warn on imprecise kind annotations ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5362](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5362)), store jkinds in typedast ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5417](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5417)), and `Sort.set` interface simplification ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5419](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5419))
- **chamelon minimizer** saw three merged PRs: module-based minimization representation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5356](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5356)), subcommands ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5421](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5421)), and build system fix ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5401](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5401)) by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp)
- **oxcaml/merlin signature-help** work: three open PRs from [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) covering labelled arguments ([oxcaml/merlin#205](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/205)), optional argument suggestions ([oxcaml/merlin#216](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/216)), and unfinished let-bindings ([oxcaml/merlin#215](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/215)); two pending upstream [ocaml/merlin#2032](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2032)
- **`ocamlfilt` demangler** in pure OCaml under continued review ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5100](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5100)) with discussion on whether to retain code pointer suffixes in demangled names
- **Dead exception handler bug** reported ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5408](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5408)): Flambda2 Simplify pass fails to remove exception handlers when the local exception optimisation triggers
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **js_of_ocaml patch elimination**: [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) merged [oxcaml/opam-repository#31](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/31) to switch `js_of_ocaml-compiler.6.0.1+ox` to track the oxcaml branch of [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) directly; this is motivated in part by parameterised library support needed for incremental jsoo compilation in dune ([ocaml/dune#13414](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13414)), connecting oxcaml/opam-repository to js_of_ocaml upstream and dune development
- **Compiler cherry-pick into merlin**: [oxcaml/merlin#218](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/218) cherry-picks changes from [oxcaml/oxcaml#5398](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5398) ahead of the normal compiler roll to fix verbose kind hover output; the same [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) who works on the type system in oxcaml/oxcaml reviewed the cherry-pick in oxcaml/merlin
- **Upstream OCaml 5.4 merge** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5325](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5325)) involves dynlink emancipation from compilerlibs ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5420](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5420)), which would affect downstream packages in oxcaml/opam-repository and janestreet/opam-repository when the merge lands
- **oxcaml-dev opam package** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5344](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5344)) provides a simplified development switch setup that bridges oxcaml/oxcaml build requirements with the oxcaml opam overlay
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **Fiber cache correctness**: [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic) identified subtle liveness issues in the cross-domain fiber return implementation in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5363](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5363), including a potential infinite loop in the cache-disabling path; [Max Slater](https://github.com/TheNumbat) iterated through multiple fixes
- **GC prefetch semantics**: [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan) in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5416](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5416) explains that `prefetchw` takes an exclusive cache-line lock, serializing domains that share objects, while `prefetchr` avoids this; a benchmark showed over 2× slowdown with `prefetchw` in multi-domain shared-object scenarios
- **dynlink ABI trade-offs**: In [oxcaml/oxcaml#5420](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5420), [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) describes how `dynu_defines` header entries as mangled symbol strings allow natdynlink to avoid depending on `Symbol.t` while preserving `ocamlobjinfo` compatibility
- **`Sort.set` interface vs. allocation**: [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin) in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5419](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5419) measured that the `Option.iter` call in the old interface is actually inlined (no closure allocation), making the new `t`-taking interface slightly more allocating; the thread discusses whether simplicity outweighs the small regression
- **Preemption continuation sizing**: [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan) and [Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark) in [oxcaml/oxcaml#4881](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4881) discuss GC root scanning implications of preemptions at non-call GC points, including how continuation objects must be dynamically sized to store `gc_regs`
- **Upstream dependency coordination for signature help**: discussion on [oxcaml/merlin#205](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/205) and [oxcaml/merlin#216](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/216) shows oxcaml-specific fixes being held until [ocaml/merlin#2032](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2032) lands upstream
- **js_of_ocaml maintainer coordination**: [oxcaml/opam-repository#31](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/31) cc'd [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) as upstream js_of_ocaml maintainer on the decision to point directly to the public oxcaml branch
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **arm64 feature parity** advancing across multiple areas: vec256 via tuple translation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5291](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5291)), test enablement ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5385](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5385)), boxed vector tag unification ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5410](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5410)), and CFG CI coverage for arm64 ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5411](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5411))
- **CFG backend correctness** receiving systematic attention from [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc) across five merged PRs this week ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5296](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5296), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5375](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5375), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5182](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5182), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5388](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5388), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5390](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5390)); the pattern of adding invariant checks before features reduces the bug surface
- **chamelon tooling maturation** with three consecutive PRs this week (module minimization, subcommands, dune integration) suggesting it is becoming a primary debugging workflow ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5356](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5356), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5421](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5421), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5423](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5423))
- **Small integer type ecosystem** growing: indexing primitives ([oxcaml/oxcaml#4779](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4779)), bit-count operations ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5393](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5393)), and x86 intrinsics ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5407](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5407)) collectively build out `int8`/`int16` unboxed type support
- **Flambda2 middle-end testability** improving through fexpr testsuite integration ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5413](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5413)) and `-dfexpr-annot` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5210](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5210)), enabling regression tests for optimizer passes
- **Reducing local patch burden** in the OxCaml opam overlay: eliminating 47 jsoo patches ([oxcaml/opam-repository#31](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/31)) continues a pattern of upstreaming OxCaml-specific changes and consuming them via branch pins
- **Eager compiler cherry-picks into merlin** ([oxcaml/merlin#218](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/218)) suggest a pattern of targeted backports to deliver user-visible fixes without waiting for bulk compiler rolls

group_summaries.brief_summary: GC idle phase landed, String.drop_prefix/suffix added, GADT errors improved, and RFC #59 on val declarations discussed.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: New String.drop_prefix/drop_suffix, GC idle phase, improved GADT error messages, and lazy structured constant optimization landed.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **`String.drop_prefix` and `String.drop_suffix`** added to the stdlib, returning `Some t` when the string matches the given prefix/suffix or `None` otherwise ([ocaml/ocaml#14487](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14487))
- **Major GC idle phase** merged: adds an `Idle` phase allowing the GC to skip unnecessary work between cycles, addressing CPU usage regressions for OCaml 5.x workloads ([ocaml/ocaml#14365](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14365))
- **Improved GADT error messages** now include hints about type variables introduced by GADT equations when they appear in error output without context (e.g. `$0`, `$1`, `$2`) ([ocaml/ocaml#14142](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14142))
- **`lazy v` optimization for structured constants**: expressions like `lazy (Cons (x, s))` are now optimized at compile time, achieving ~2.7x speedup in benchmarks vs. OCaml 5.3 ([ocaml/ocaml#13919](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13919))
- **`ocamltest` conditionals** `&&`, `||`, and `if/then/else` added to the test statement language for platform-conditional test setup ([ocaml/ocaml#14532](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14532))
- **`ocamltest` `system` variable** introduced, exposing the configure-time system name (e.g. `macosx`, `linux`, `freebsd`) for use in test conditions ([ocaml/ocaml#14545](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14545))
- **Inclusion check error messages** improved: error messages now reference the original source file rather than ppx-generated artifacts ([ocaml/ocaml#14205](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14205))
- **`git-archive` export-ignore** merged: removes testsuite, manual, and other files from GitHub-generated source archives, shrinking opam installation archives by ~25% and saving ~100 seconds on first-time Windows installs ([ocaml/ocaml#14152](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14152))
- **`Scannable_*` macros** replace raw `No_scan_tag` comparisons throughout the runtime, reducing direction/equality errors and fixing a latent bug in `weak.c` ([ocaml/ocaml#14493](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14493))
- **`Load_path.remove_dir` bug fixed** in the toplevel: calling `#remove_directory` previously destroyed the `auto_include_callback`, causing `open Str` to fail after directory manipulation ([ocaml/ocaml#14551](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14551))
group_summaries.group_overview:
- Activity in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) was broad, spanning GC improvements, stdlib additions, tooling overhauls, type system fixes, and platform-specific backend work
- [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs) saw focused discussion on [ocaml/RFCs#59](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/59) (`val` declarations in structures), with debate around shadowing semantics and bidirectional type checking implications
