- Key merges in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) this week include the GC idle phase ([ocaml/ocaml#14365](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14365)), `String.drop_prefix/suffix` ([ocaml/ocaml#14487](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14487)), `lazy` structured constant optimization ([ocaml/ocaml#13919](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13919)), and `ocamltest` DSL extensions ([ocaml/ocaml#14532](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14532), [ocaml/ocaml#14545](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14545))
- Fourteen contributors were active in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml); [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) also connects both repositories as the author of [ocaml/RFCs#59](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/59) and a reviewer in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml)
- OxCaml upstream contributions visible in several merged PRs: [ocaml/ocaml#14493](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14493), [ocaml/ocaml#14492](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14492), and [ocaml/ocaml#14205](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14205)
group_summaries.activity_summary: New PRs opened for Atomic.Array, Fun.todo, s390x backend fixes, CFI on Power, and a multi-PR typing refactor series by johnyob.
group_summaries.activity:
- **Newly opened: `Atomic.Array` module** ([ocaml/ocaml#14380](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14380)) proposed by [@clef-men](https://github.com/clef-men) and [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), implementing atomic arrays over atomic record fields machinery with `make`, `init`, `get`, `set`, `exchange`, `compare_and_set`, and `fetch_and_add` operations; awaiting stdlib API second approval
- **Newly opened: `Fun.todo` with code location payload** ([ocaml/ocaml#14525](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14525)) by [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole), adding `Fun.todo : unit -> 'a` backed by a new `Todo of todo` builtin exception with an abstract location type
- **Newly opened: `Array.fold_left2` and `Array.fold_right2`** ([ocaml/ocaml#14553](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14553)) by [PetitFrapo](https://github.com/PetitFrapo) to match existing `List` module equivalents
- **Newly opened: s390x native `Iatomic_fetch_add`** ([ocaml/ocaml#14524](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14524)) using the `LAAG` instruction by [Zane Hambly](https://github.com/Zaneham), with TSan instrumentation
- **Newly opened: s390x GOT-indirect calls fix** ([ocaml/ocaml#14547](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14547)) by [Zane Hambly](https://github.com/Zaneham) to prevent heap corruption when shared runtime `libasmrun_shared.so` PLT trampolines run on the OCaml fiber stack
- **Newly opened: CFI support for Power architecture** ([ocaml/ocaml#14482](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14482)) by [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist), enabling GDB/profiler backtraces on ppc64le
- **Newly opened: `Fix Obj.dup` on closures** ([ocaml/ocaml#14492](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14492)) by [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes), handling closures and infix tags in `caml_obj_with_tag`, upstreamed from OxCaml
- **Newly opened: typing refactor series** by [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob) splitting `type_let` ([ocaml/ocaml#14394](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14394)), refactoring `type_approx` for level-awareness ([ocaml/ocaml#14538](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14538)), supporting locally-abstract type approximation in `let rec` ([ocaml/ocaml#14517](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14517)), and approximating modular explicits in `let rec` ([ocaml/ocaml#14537](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14537))
- **Newly opened: typechecker error trace refactor** ([ocaml/ocaml#14536](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14536)) by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron), introducing a structured type for error traces to separate post-processing from printing
- **Newly opened: `LN_S`, `AR`, `ARFLAGS` in configure and Makefiles** ([ocaml/ocaml#14552](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14552)) by [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA), replacing `ARCMD` with idiomatic GNU Make variables
- **Ongoing: `Fix PUSHENVACC4` opcode** ([ocaml/ocaml#14534](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14534)) by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo): a `n < 4` vs `n <= 4` off-by-one caused the 1-word `PUSHENVACC4` opcode to never be emitted; found via AI-assisted review
- **Ongoing: `Always keep names`** typing PR ([ocaml/ocaml#13328](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13328)) by [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) to preserve type variable names through `copy` operations
- **Ongoing: Haiku native backend** ([ocaml/ocaml#14138](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14138)) by [Anarchos](https://github.com/Sylvain78) to enable the native compiler on Haiku OS
- **RFC discussion: `val` declarations in structures** ([ocaml/RFCs#59](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/59)) received new comments this week on shadowing semantics and implications for bidirectional type checking
- **Closed without merge: `atomic record fields [%atomic.field ...]`** alternative approach ([ocaml/ocaml#13707](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13707)) closed by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) now that `[%atomic.loc ...]` ([ocaml/ocaml#13404](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13404)) has merged
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) is active in both repositories: he is the author of [ocaml/RFCs#59](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/59) on `val` declarations, and this week merged the `lazy` structured constant optimization ([ocaml/ocaml#13919](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13919)) and `ocamltest` conditionals ([ocaml/ocaml#14532](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14532)) in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml)
- The RFC [ocaml/RFCs#59](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/59) on `val` declarations is conceptually linked to ongoing typechecking improvements in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml), including [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob)'s `let rec` approximation series ([ocaml/ocaml#14517](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14517), [ocaml/ocaml#14537](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14537)) and [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron)'s error trace restructuring ([ocaml/ocaml#14536](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14536))
- The `val` shadowing semantics discussed in [ocaml/RFCs#59](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/59) have direct implications for bidirectional type propagation from `.mli` files to `.ml` implementations, a direction that several [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) typing PRs are incrementally enabling
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **`Fun.todo` API design** ([ocaml/ocaml#14525](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14525)) generated discussion on encoding call location in the exception payload: [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) argued for payload inclusion for cases without backtraces, [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) wanted an abstract type to avoid committing to a representation, and [Yawar Amin](https://github.com/yawaramin) argued for a simple `Todo of string` with manual `~__LOC__` passing; the PR now uses an abstract `todo` type marked deprecated
- **GC pacing defaults in OCaml 5.5** ([ocaml/ocaml#14533](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14533)) discussed after [Romain Beauxis](https://github.com/toots) reported significantly higher CPU usage than 4.14 for streaming workloads with large custom blocks; `custom_major_ratio = 250` was found to restore near-4.14 behavior, and the merged GC idle phase ([ocaml/ocaml#14365](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14365)) is expected to help further
- **`let module` value restriction regression** ([ocaml/ocaml#14554](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14554)) reported by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) in OCaml 5.5 related to [ocaml/ocaml#13839](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13839); [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) confirmed it should not break previously-typechecking code and looped in [Samuel Vivien](https://github.com/samsa1) and [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche)
- **AI-assisted bug finding** highlighted in multiple PRs: [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) used Claude to find the `PUSHENVACC4` off-by-one ([ocaml/ocaml#14534](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14534)) and several other bytecode optimizations, generating discussion on test methodology for AI-discovered fixes
- **RFC #59 `val` shadowing semantics** ([ocaml/RFCs#59](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/59)): [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan) raised concerns that the RFC is ambiguous about whether `val` can be shadowed by a plain `let` or `include`, arguing that disallowing shadowing would make bidirectional type propagation practical; [Nicolas Jeannerod](https://github.com/Niols) responded that `val` should not be shadowable, interpreting it as attached to the last binding of that name
- **Readable multi-line quoted string literals** ([ocaml/ocaml#13860](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13860)) saw renewed discussion with [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) proposing `{foo\ ... |foo}` syntax and [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) discussing the `odoc` specification for stripping leading whitespace
- **Floating attributes with `-no-alias-deps` ignored** ([ocaml/ocaml#11867](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11867)) saw [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) propose an `[@@inherit alert]` attribute on module aliases as a potential solution
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **s390x architecture receiving sustained attention**: three separate PRs this week from [Zane Hambly](https://github.com/Zaneham) ([ocaml/ocaml#14524](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14524), [ocaml/ocaml#14547](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14547), [ocaml/ocaml#14514](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14514)) plus an existing CFI fix ([ocaml/ocaml#14500](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14500)) show growing community effort to bring s390x to full feature parity with other native backends
- **AI-assisted code review** becoming visible in the codebase: both the `PUSHENVACC4` fix ([ocaml/ocaml#14534](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14534)) and the subsequently closed `Complex.sqrt` investigation ([ocaml/ocaml#14535](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14535)) were found by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) using Claude, prompting discussion about test methodology for AI-discovered fixes
- **Typing infrastructure refactoring** gaining momentum with a multi-PR series by [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob) ([ocaml/ocaml#14394](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14394), [ocaml/ocaml#14538](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14538), [ocaml/ocaml#14517](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14517), [ocaml/ocaml#14537](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14537)) targeting `let rec` approximation, alongside [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron)'s error trace restructuring ([ocaml/ocaml#14536](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14536)) and the RFC-level discussion in [ocaml/RFCs#59](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/59) on `val` declarations
- **`ocamltest` DSL expansion** continuing: `if/then/else` and `&&`/`||` conditionals ([ocaml/ocaml#14532](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14532)) and the new `system` variable ([ocaml/ocaml#14545](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14545)) show incremental improvement of the test framework to reduce boilerplate in platform-specific tests
- **OxCaml upstream contributions** visible in several merged PRs: `Scannable_*` macros ([ocaml/ocaml#14493](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14493)), `Fix Obj.dup` on closures ([ocaml/ocaml#14492](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14492)), and inclusion check error improvement ([ocaml/ocaml#14205](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14205)) were all noted as upstreamed from `oxcaml/oxcaml`

group_summaries.brief_summary: Activity this week was limited to ocaml-multicore/meio, which landed Eio 0.14+ compatibility via a migration from deprecated private APIs.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: meio migrated to Eio_runtime_events and nottui-unix, enabling installation from the standard opam repository.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **Migration from `Eio.Private.Ctf` to `Eio_runtime_events`** in [ocaml-multicore/meio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio): the internal event callback system was rewritten to use the new `Eio_runtime_events` module and its typed `event` variant, replacing the older `Ctf`-based callback approach with a unified `RE.add_callbacks` call ([ocaml-multicore/meio#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72), [673fdbd](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/commit/673fdbd110a5695b1ed297d938e1a27233464c68))
- **Switch from `nottui` to `nottui-unix`** in [ocaml-multicore/meio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio): the UI event loop now uses `Nottui_unix.run` instead of `Nottui.Ui_loop.run`, reflecting an upstream split of the nottui library ([ocaml-multicore/meio#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72), [673fdbd](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/commit/673fdbd110a5695b1ed297d938e1a27233464c68))
- **New `eio_name` helper library for examples**: a small `Eio_name` library extracted into `example/eio_name.ml` provides a `name` function using `Eio.Private.Trace.name`, replacing direct use of `Eio.Private.Ctf.set_name` and `Eio.Private.Ctf.log` in example programs ([ocaml-multicore/meio#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72), [673fdbd](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/commit/673fdbd110a5695b1ed297d938e1a27233464c68))
- **opam dependency updates**: `meio.opam` now requires `eio >= 0.14`, `nottui-unix` (replacing `nottui`), and `dune >= 3.10`; the custom git pin-depends on a private eio fork have been removed, enabling installation from the standard opam repository ([ocaml-multicore/meio#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72), [673fdbd](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/commit/673fdbd110a5695b1ed297d938e1a27233464c68))
- **Improved task kind display**: `console.ml` now renders task kinds using `Eio_runtime_events.pp_event` for unknown event types, and correctly distinguishes cancellation contexts (`cc`) from fibers (`task`) using new variant patterns from `Eio_runtime_events.event` ([ocaml-multicore/meio#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72), [673fdbd](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/commit/673fdbd110a5695b1ed297d938e1a27233464c68))
- **Removed `Ctf.with_tracing` requirement from examples**: `burn.ml`, `burn_domains.ml`, `main.ml`, and `zero.ml` no longer need to wrap execution in `Ctf.with_tracing`, simplifying example entry points ([ocaml-multicore/meio#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72), [673fdbd](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/commit/673fdbd110a5695b1ed297d938e1a27233464c68))
- **CI updated to OCaml 5.3**: the CI workflow now targets OCaml 5.3 (from 5.1.1) and uses `ocaml/setup-ocaml@v3`, removing custom opam repository overrides and the custom notty pin ([ocaml-multicore/meio#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72), [673fdbd](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/commit/673fdbd110a5695b1ed297d938e1a27233464c68))
group_summaries.group_overview:
- Activity this week was confined to [ocaml-multicore/meio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio); all other repositories (eio, domainslib, ocaml-uring, saturn, eio-trace, picos, kcas, ocaml-iomux) recorded no commits, PRs, or issues
- [ocaml-multicore/meio#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72) landed Eio 0.14+ compatibility, bringing meio up to date with current Eio API surfaces and removing reliance on `Eio.Private.Ctf` internal APIs
- [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) rebased [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris)'s earlier work from [ocaml-multicore/meio#71](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/71) and merged it as [ocaml-multicore/meio#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72) (22 files changed)
- A follow-up issue [ocaml-multicore/meio#73](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/73) was opened to track re-enabling screencast generation in CI, which was disabled due to a dependency on a patched `notty` library hardcoding terminal size
group_summaries.activity_summary: meio merged Eio 0.14+ compatibility and opened a follow-up issue to re-enable screencast CI generation.
group_summaries.activity:
- [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) merged [ocaml-multicore/meio#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72) "Eio.0.14+", a rebased version of [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris)'s earlier work in [ocaml-multicore/meio#71](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/71), bringing the codebase up to date with the modern Eio API surface
- [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) opened [ocaml-multicore/meio#73](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/73) "Re-enable the screencast generation in CI", noting that the screencast pipeline depended on a patched version of the `notty` library that hardcoded terminal size; the screencast steps are currently commented out in CI pending either an upstream fix or a workaround for terminal-size configuration
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- The meio Eio 0.14+ update ([ocaml-multicore/meio#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72)) directly tracks the `Eio_runtime_events` API stabilised in [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) 0.14+, illustrating how downstream tools in the group must follow Eio's evolving public API surface
- The removal of the private git pin-depends on a custom eio fork in `meio.opam` (part of [ocaml-multicore/meio#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72)) unblocks standard opam installation, reducing friction for users of the broader multicore tooling ecosystem
group_summaries.notable_discussions: - [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) commented on [ocaml-multicore/meio#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72) thanking [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) for rebasing and landing the Eio 0.14+ compatibility work originally authored in [ocaml-multicore/meio#71](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/71)
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Eio API modernisation across the group**: the migration in [ocaml-multicore/meio#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72) from `Eio.Private.Ctf` to `Eio_runtime_events` reflects stabilisation of the runtime-events interface in Eio 0.14+; other tools in the group that still reference `Eio.Private.*` APIs may face similar migration work
- **CI infrastructure debt**: the screencast generation pipeline in meio was disabled rather than updated ([ocaml-multicore/meio#73](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/73)), highlighting a recurring pattern where documentation and tooling CI falls behind library dependency evolution

weekly_summary.brief_summary: 3 releases (dune 3.21.1, js_of_ocaml 6.3.1/6.3.2), 34 OxCaml PRs, 22+ ecosystem package releases, and meio modernization.
weekly_summary.new_features_summary: GC idle phase, String.drop_prefix/suffix, SIMD int8/int16 intrinsics, dune sandboxing defaults, and ocaml-lsp build-system-agnostic config landed this week.
weekly_summary.new_features:
- __RUMINANT:core__ **GC idle phase** merged, allowing the GC to perform idle-time work [ocaml/ocaml#14365](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14365)
- __RUMINANT:core__ **`String.drop_prefix`** and **`String.drop_suffix`** added to the standard library [ocaml/ocaml#14487](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14487)
- __RUMINANT:core__ **`lazy v` structured constant optimization** delivering ~2.7x speedup for lazy values applied to constants [ocaml/ocaml#13919](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13919)
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Improved GADT error messages** for better type-error diagnostics [ocaml/ocaml#14142](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14142)
- __RUMINANT:core__ **`ocamltest` conditional expressions** (`&&`, `||`, `if/then/else`) added for richer test scripting [ocaml/ocaml#14532](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14532)
- __RUMINANT:core__ **`ocamltest` `system` variable** added for cross-platform test conditions [ocaml/ocaml#14545](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14545)
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Inclusion check error messages** improved for module type mismatches [ocaml/ocaml#14205](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14205)
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Haiku OS native backend** added for the native-code compiler [ocaml/ocaml#14138](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14138) by [Anarchos](https://github.com/Sylvain78)
- __RUMINANT:core__ **CFI (Call Frame Information) for Power architecture** enabling better debugging and unwinding [ocaml/ocaml#14482](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14482) by [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Abstract kinds (part 2)** implementation advancing the kind system in OxCaml [oxcaml/oxcaml#5071](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5071)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **SIMD indexing primitives for small integers** (int8, int16) with x86 intrinsics [oxcaml/oxcaml#4779](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4779) and [oxcaml/oxcaml#5407](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5407)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **`ctz`, `clz`, `popcnt` operations for small int types** [oxcaml/oxcaml#5393](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5393)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **`[%%expect_asm]` test support** for assembly-level testing [oxcaml/oxcaml#5350](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5350)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **`chamelon run` and `chamelon dune` subcommands** extending the chamelon tooling for project management [oxcaml/oxcaml#5421](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5421) and [oxcaml/oxcaml#5423](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5423)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Manual module initialization** support [oxcaml/oxcaml#5395](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5395)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Wide vectors (vec256) on arm64** via tuple translation [oxcaml/oxcaml#5291](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5291)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Layout-polymorphic bindings** support [oxcaml/oxcaml#5392](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5392)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **GC prefetch fix** improving multi-domain performance [oxcaml/oxcaml#5416](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5416)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Fiber cache domain-affinity fix** for safer concurrency [oxcaml/oxcaml#5363](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5363)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **`-dfexpr-annot` compiler flag** for annotated Flambda expression debugging [oxcaml/oxcaml#5210](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5210)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **`runtime5` in `ocamlc -config`** output [oxcaml/oxcaml#5415](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5415)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Merlin signature help** feature ported to OxCaml's Merlin fork [oxcaml/merlin#214](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/214) and [oxcaml/merlin#219](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/219)
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Dune sandboxing defaults** enabled for tests and Melange rules [ocaml/dune#13617](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13617) and [ocaml/dune#13619](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13619)
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **odoc-config.sexp generation** for cross-package documentation support [ocaml/dune#13476](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13476)

- __RUMINANT:tools__ **BitSet CTZ (count-trailing-zeros) optimization** for faster bit operations in js_of_ocaml [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2179](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2179)
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **mkernel 0.0.1** new package for OCaml kernel module development [ocaml/opam-repository#29361](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29361) by [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure)
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **utcp 0.0.1** new userspace TCP stack package [ocaml/opam-repository#29359](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29359) by [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm)
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **virtfs 1.0.0** and **yocaml 3.0.0** released [ocaml/opam-repository#29381](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29381) and [ocaml/opam-repository#29377](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29377) by [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw)
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **OCaml Planet scraper** restructured with scheduled scraping workflows [ocaml/ocaml.org#3503](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3503) and [ocaml/ocaml.org#3508](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3508)
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ **meio modernized** with migration from `Eio.Private.Ctf` to `Eio_runtime_events` and switch from `nottui` to `nottui-unix` [ocaml-multicore/meio#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72)

weekly_summary.group_overview:
- __RUMINANT:core__ The **core OCaml compiler** saw 14 contributors land improvements across the GC, standard library, and testing infrastructure, with the **GC idle phase** [ocaml/ocaml#14365](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14365) and **`lazy` constant optimization** [ocaml/ocaml#13919](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13919) being the most impactful merged features; platform support broadened with Haiku [ocaml/ocaml#14138](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14138) and Power CFI [ocaml/ocaml#14482](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14482) additions.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ OxCaml had one of its busiest weeks with 34 merged PRs across [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml), advancing **SIMD support** for small integer types [oxcaml/oxcaml#5407](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5407), the **abstract kinds** system [oxcaml/oxcaml#5071](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5071), and the **chamelon** developer toolchain [oxcaml/oxcaml#5421](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5421); an upstream OCaml 5.4 merge series [oxcaml/oxcaml#5325](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5325), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5376](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5376), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5420](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5420) continued in parallel.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ The **tools** group shipped **dune 3.21.1** [ocaml/dune#13557](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13557) as a patch release fixing shell completion installation [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2186](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2186), while merging **sandboxing defaults** for tests and Melange rules [ocaml/dune#13617](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13617) and a **memo deadlock fix** [ocaml/dune#13625](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13625); **js_of_ocaml 6.3.1 and 6.3.2** were released as patch fixes, and **ocaml-lsp** gained build-system-agnostic configuration [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1581](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1581).
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ The **ecosystem** saw 22+ new package releases including new networking packages **mkernel** [ocaml/opam-repository#29361](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29361) and **utcp** [ocaml/opam-repository#29359](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29359), **dune 3.21.1** [ocaml/opam-repository#29374](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29374), **menhir 20260209** [ocaml/opam-repository#29364](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29364), and **containers 3.18** [ocaml/opam-repository#29372](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29372); [Bozhidar Batsov](https://github.com/bbatsov) contributed a large batch of documentation quality improvements to ocaml.org fixing 100 typos and 469 markdownlint violations.
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ The **multicore** group had focused activity on the **meio** monitoring tool, modernizing its Eio integration by migrating from the private `Eio.Private.Ctf` API to the public `Eio_runtime_events` interface [ocaml-multicore/meio#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72) and upgrading CI to OCaml 5.3.

weekly_summary.cross_repository_work:
- __RUMINANT:tools__ and __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ coordinated on the **dune 3.21.1 patch release** [ocaml/dune#13557](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13557): the release was triggered by a shell completion installation regression in js_of_ocaml [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2186](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2186), requiring coordinated publication across [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune), [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) (6.3.1 and 6.3.2), and [ocaml/opam-repository#29374](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29374).
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ and __RUMINANT:tools__ coordinated on **js_of_ocaml OxCaml support**: 47 OxCaml patches were eliminated from js_of_ocaml's patch set (tracked in [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2105](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2105)), with OxCaml js_of_ocaml opam packages published to [oxcaml/opam-repository#31](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/31).
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **upstream OCaml 5.4 merge** series [oxcaml/oxcaml#5325](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5325), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5376](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5376), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5403](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5403), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5420](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5420) continues pulling changes from [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) into [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml), maintaining the divergence at a manageable level.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ and __RUMINANT:core__ share the **Merlin signature help** feature: work in [oxcaml/merlin#205](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/205) by [Charlène Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) has an upstream counterpart at [ocaml/merlin#2032](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2032).
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **js_of_ocaml incremental build** improvements in [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) ([ocaml/dune#13611](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13611)–[ocaml/dune#13621](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13621)) coordinated with optimizations in [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon).
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **dune odoc-config generation** [ocaml/dune#13476](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13476) connecting dune build output with odoc's cross-package documentation system, closing [ocaml/dune#13485](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13485).

weekly_summary.notable_discussions:
- __RUMINANT:core__ **`Fun.todo` API design** debated in [ocaml/ocaml#14525](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14525) by [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole): the proposal adds a `Fun.todo : unit -> 'a` placeholder raising `Not_implemented` at runtime, with discussion about naming and semantics versus `failwith`.
- __RUMINANT:core__ **GC pacing defaults** discussion initiated by [Romain Beauxis](https://github.com/toots) in [ocaml/ocaml#14533](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14533) following the GC idle phase merge, examining whether default GC parameters are appropriate for production workloads.
- __RUMINANT:core__ **`let module` and the value restriction** raised by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) in [ocaml/ocaml#14554](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14554), questioning whether local module expressions should be subject to the value restriction in the same way as regular expressions.
- __RUMINANT:core__ **RFC #59: `val` declarations in structures** ([ocaml/RFCs#59](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/59)) received renewed discussion from [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan) and [Nicolas Jeannerod](https://github.com/Niors) about how `val` shadowing semantics should interact with module system invariants.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Dune 3.21.1 merge strategy** discussed in [ocaml/dune#13557](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13557): the team debated backport approach for the shell completion regression, deciding on a targeted patch release rather than waiting for the next minor version.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Alerts in wrapped libraries** discussion in [ocaml/dune#13622](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13622) examining how dune should propagate or suppress OCaml alert annotations in wrapped library contexts.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Lockfile build detection** debated in [ocaml/dune#13592](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13592) by [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV), discussing how dune's rev-store should determine the correct default build command when a lockfile is present.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Variable coalescing in js_of_ocaml** [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2166](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2166) by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) demonstrating 74–91% register reduction and 9.3–16.5% output compression improvements; notable discussion about the optimization strategy.
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **Agentic PR workflow** experiment discussed in [ocaml/ocaml.org#3496](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3496) by [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac) exploring automated documentation improvement tooling for ocaml.org.
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **opam-repository immutability policy** [ocaml/opam-repository#29072](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29072) by [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) continued as an ongoing policy discussion about whether published package versions should be immutable.
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **containers 3.18 breaking changes** discussed in [ocaml/opam-repository#29372](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29372) as [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) published the new version, with dependent packages needing updates.

weekly_summary.emerging_trends:
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **OxCaml SIMD and low-level optimization** is accelerating: week 7 added int8/int16 SIMD intrinsics [oxcaml/oxcaml#5407](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5407), vec256 on arm64 [oxcaml/oxcaml#5291](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5291), and bit matrix register allocation [oxcaml/oxcaml#5296](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5296), continuing the trend from weeks 5–6 of expanding the SIMD ISA coverage.
- __RUMINANT:core__ and __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **GC improvements across both compiler branches**: the upstream GC idle phase [ocaml/ocaml#14365](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14365) complements OxCaml's GC prefetch fix [oxcaml/oxcaml#5416](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5416) and fiber cache domain-affinity fix [oxcaml/oxcaml#5363](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5363), indicating coordinated attention to GC performance across both codebases.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Dune sandboxing becoming default**: after weeks of preparation, sandboxing is now enabled by default for tests [ocaml/dune#13617](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13617) and Melange rules [ocaml/dune#13619](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13619), a security and correctness improvement that will affect all dune users upgrading to 3.21+.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **js_of_ocaml optimization focus**: variable coalescing [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2166](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2166), CTZ-based BitSet [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2179](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2179), subst speedup [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2185](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2185), and block sharing [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2184](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2184) represent a concentrated push to reduce output size and improve runtime performance.
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **opam-repository housekeeping at scale**: the ongoing archival initiative [ocaml/opam-repository#29058](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29058) touching ~46,000 files of unmaintained packages reflects a broader effort to keep the package index manageable, continuing the trend seen in previous weeks.
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **New infrastructure-level OCaml packages**: the emergence of **mkernel** [ocaml/opam-repository#29361](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29361) and **utcp** [ocaml/opam-repository#29359](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29359) from the MirageOS ecosystem signals continued investment in unikernel and systems-level OCaml development.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **LSP ecosystem independence**: the build-system-agnostic merlin configuration in ocaml-lsp [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1581](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1581) via environment variables indicates a trend toward making OCaml tooling work outside the standard dune workflow.
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Platform breadth expansion**: Haiku backend [ocaml/ocaml#14138](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14138), s390x `Iatomic_fetch_add` [ocaml/ocaml#14524](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14524), and Power CFI [ocaml/ocaml#14482](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14482) in a single week shows ongoing broadening of OCaml's platform support matrix.
